target.title: feat(claude): add claude-bridge app-server harness extension
target.body:
## Summary

TL;DR:
Inspiration: https://openclaw.ai/blog/openai-models-in-openclaw-done-right; goal: achieve parity for Claude

New feature; issue https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/93793

OpenClaw currently has no first-class harness for Anthropic models. Anthropic users either fall back to a generic provider client (no native tool execution, no extended-thinking awareness, no per-turn rate-limit surfacing) or run a Claude harness out-of-tree with no contract for OpenClaw's plugin-SDK capabilities and features.

The codex harness already ships in-tree with the same shape we want ŌĆö `extensions/codex/src/app-server/` is a codex-app-server JSON-RPC client over stdio. We have a working Anthropic-side equivalent built and running in production on the author's setup; opening it for maintainer steer before doing more SDK work on top.

Harness: this PR
JSON-RPC server: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@zeroaltitude/openclaw-claude-bridge

**What is the intended outcome?**

- Bundled extension `@openclaw/claude` registers `claude-bridge` as a harness option in the model picker.
- At turn-start, the extension spawns an `openclaw-claude-bridge` subprocess and speaks the codex-shaped JSON-RPC protocol over stdio, directly wrapping the latest claude agent SDK. The server (currently published as `@zeroaltitude/openclaw-claude-bridge`  https://www.npmjs.com/package/@zeroaltitude/openclaw-claude-bridge ŌĆö fork-preview path; would migrate to `@openclaw/openclaw-claude-bridge` if the maintainers republish it) handles Anthropic API I/O, rate-limit surfacing, image-payload sanitization, and plugin-thread-config persistence.
- Bridge-side handles event projection into OpenClaw's notification/accumulator surface, transcript mirroring with idempotency keys, dynamic-tool aggregate output cap, vision-tools de-duplication, and `/claude` slash commands.
- See `extensions/claude/README.md` for the full feature map.

**What is intentionally out of scope?**

- Republishing the server under `@openclaw/openclaw-claude-bridge` ŌĆö needs maintainer decision; the `@zeroaltitude` scope dependency is the current placeholder.
- Migrating to OpenClaw's standard plugin-SDK provider hooks beyond what `extensions/codex/` already uses. The bridge follows codex's pattern verbatim where applicable.
- Anything outside the harness bridge surface.

**What does success look like?** Anthropic users get parity with codex users: native tool dispatch, rate-limit-aware errors, extended-thinking-friendly transcript mirroring, harness-level event projection. No regressions to existing harnesses.

**What should reviewers focus on?**

1. **Scope concern ŌĆö am I taking too much core?** The branch touches `src/plugins/command-registration.ts`, `src/plugins/current-plugin-metadata-snapshot.ts`, `src/plugins/agent-tool-result-middleware-types.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.ts`, `src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.ts`, and `src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts`. Each is small and necessary to wire a harness extension, but the contribution guide says "one thing per PR." Happy to split into prep PRs first if maintainers prefer.
2. **External-plugin vs. bundled-extension** ŌĆö Per CONTRIBUTING.md "most features should be third-party plugins." Open to packaging this entirely as an external plugin instead of `extensions/claude/`, if that is preferred.
3. **Anthropic-API behavior delegated to a separate server repo** ŌĆö Most of the actual API logic lives in the server (https://github.com/zeroaltitude/openclaw-plugins/tree/main/openclaw-claude/server -- https://www.npmjs.com/package/@zeroaltitude/openclaw-claude-bridge). Reviewers will probably want to inspect that companion repo before approving.
4. **`claude-bridge` harness id naming** ŌĆö there's no precedent for "<vendor>-bridge" harness names; renamable. (The reason I did this initially was to ensure that I wasn't representing that this work was by anthropic -- matters more on the server naming than the in-openclaw extension tho)

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Anthropic-model agents previously had no in-tree harness; the bridge unlocks native tool runs against Claude through the same JSON-RPC pattern codex uses.
- **Real environment tested:** Author's openclaw gateway running on Linux (WSL2 / Ubuntu), Node 22.x. Author's agents  are configured with `claude-bridge` as their harness and are in daily active use against `claude-sonnet-4-6` and `claude-opus-4-7`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. `pnpm install && pnpm build`
  2. `openclaw gateway restart --deep`
  3. Send a Slack DM to one of the claude-bridge agents
  4. Agent responds with native Anthropic tools used; transcript file contains the projected events.
  5. `/claude status` in the same thread prints shared-client liveness.
- **Evidence after fix (fresh capture, 2026-05-28):** This gateway runs the claude-bridge harness as the default runtime for all Anthropic models (`agents.defaults.models.*.agentRuntime.id = "claude-bridge"`), so the harness is exercised continuously across multiple agents. Captured live:
  1. **Bridge liveness** ŌĆö shared-client lifecycle today: `claude-bridge: spawned ├Ś5 / initialized ├Ś5 / process exited ├Ś5` (restarts and re-initializes without manual intervention).
  2. **A turn completing with visible tool/progress output** ŌĆö one bridge turn dispatching consecutive tools, each completing, with per-tool provenance timing from the observability plugin: `ToolSearch ŌåÆ after_tool_call 1.9ms`, `exec ŌåÆ 1.7ms`, `Write ŌåÆ 1.9ms`, `Agent ŌåÆ 1.9ms`.
  3. **Transcript / result event stream** ŌĆö the run persists a projected event stream to `~/.openclaw/agents/<id>/sessions/<uuid>.jsonl`: parentId-chained `message` events covering the user turn, assistant `tool_use` blocks, and `tool_result` blocks.
  4. **`/claude status`** (live, from a bridge-backed agent):
     ```
     Claude app-server status
     Shared client: running
     Command: openclaw-claude-bridge
     Last stderr: [turn-runner] system event {"subtype":"init"} / {"subtype":"status"} / unhandled SDK event {"type":"rate_limit_event"}
     ```
  *Log-hygiene note:* a few `claude-bridge: runAttempt failed` lines appear in the same logs; they are not bridge defects ŌĆö one class is a context-overflow on a zero-message scheduled run (auto-compaction correctly skipped), the other is transient model-call failover during an MCP reconnect; both recovered on the next turn. Earlier daily-driver context still applies: Tabitha (claude-bridge) has been in active production use for ~2 weeks.
- **Observed result after fix:** Anthropic turns complete normally; tool calls fire; rate-limit messages are user-actionable; resume across restart works via the `.claude-binding.json` sidecar.
- **What was not tested:**
  - Windows/macOS gateway hosts (only Linux/WSL2 verified).
  - Bun runtime (Node 22 only).
  - Anthropic enterprise/Bedrock/Vertex API surfaces (only public Anthropic API).
  - Concurrent multi-account scenarios beyond 3 agents.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** Author runs a single-host gateway. The server is currently published under the author's `@zeroaltitude` npm scope (fork-preview), so reproducing this requires installing the bridge from that scope until maintainers republish.
- **Before evidence:** N/A ŌĆö no prior in-tree harness for Anthropic to compare against.

## Tests and validation

- Real world use for over a month on a broad diversity of tasks with observed, anecdotal, improvements in coding, token usage and well executed long running tasks
- `pnpm build` Ō£ō (local)
- `pnpm test extensions/claude` Ō£ō (local ŌĆö extension unit tests, including event projector, dynamic-tools middleware port, thread fork policy envelope, transcript mirror dedupe)
- `pnpm test:extensions` not run since last rebase ŌĆö recommend Testbox/Crabbox run before non-draft review.
- No `CHANGELOG.md` edit per contributor rule; maintainer to add on land.

## Risk checklist

- **Did user-visible behavior change?** Yes ŌĆö new `claude-bridge` harness appears in model picker; new `/claude` slash command surface.
- **Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** Yes ŌĆö new plugin `configSchema` (see `extensions/claude/openclaw.plugin.json`). Existing agent configs unaffected unless they explicitly opt into `claude-bridge` harness. Doctor contract added (`extensions/claude/doctor-contract-api.ts`).
- **Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** Yes (intentional). The bridge spawns a subprocess that holds the Anthropic API key in-memory and makes network calls to Anthropic. Subprocess lifecycle is owned by the harness, not user shell. The plugin SDK enabler changes (e.g. `command-registration.ts`) are additive and follow existing codex-extension precedent.
- **Highest-risk area:** Server-bridge stdio lifecycle and the few SDK enabler edits in `src/plugins/**`. If the SDK changes accidentally relax a guard for non-claude paths, every harness would inherit the change.
- **How is that risk mitigated?** The SDK changes are narrowly scoped ŌĆö each follows a codex-extension-shaped precedent. Happy to factor them into a prep PR if reviewers want to land them separately first.

## Current review state

- **Next action:** Maintainer steer on scope: (a) accept as bundled extension as-is, (b) split SDK enablers into a prep PR first, or (c) request packaging as an external plugin.
- **Still waiting on:** Maintainer decision on the above. CI run on this push.
- **Bot/reviewer comments addressed:** None yet ŌĆö opening draft.

---

­¤ż¢ **AI-assisted disclosure:** Developed with the assistance of Claude Code (anthropic.com/claude-code). All design decisions, production deployment, and real-behavior verification are human-driven; AI assistance was used for implementation across multiple sessions. Session transcripts available on request.

target.title: codex: plumb session reasoningLevel into codex model_reasoning_summary
target.body:
## Problem

The codex app-server defaults `model_reasoning_summary` to `"none"` when it isnt explicitly configured. As a result codex still produces reasoning items internally (visible in its rollout `.jsonl`) but never emits `item/reasoning/summaryTextDelta` notifications. That leaves `CodexAppServerEventProjector.handleReasoningDelta` with nothing to project ŌĆö long codex turns appear silent in any UI subscribed to the gateway until the final answer arrives.

The pi-embedded runtime already maps session reasoning preferences into `reasoning.summary` via `openai-transport-stream.ts`. This brings the codex app-server path to parity.

## Repro before this change

1. Configure an agent with `agentRuntime.id: "codex"`.
2. `sessions.patch` `{ reasoningLevel: "stream" }`.
3. Send a prompt that requires multi-step reasoning.
4. Inspect `~/.openclaw/agents/<id>/agent/codex-home/sessions/.../rollout-*.jsonl`: reasoning items are present but every one has `"summary": []`. The session trajectory has zero reasoning events.

## Change

- New helper `resolveCodexReasoningSummary(level)`:
  - `"off"` ŌåÆ `"none"`
  - `"on"` / `"stream"` ŌåÆ `"auto"`
  - unset / unknown ŌåÆ `undefined` (preserves the user's `~/.codex/config.toml` or codex's own default; no behavior change for callers that haven't opted in)
- `buildThreadStartParams` and `buildThreadResumeParams` merge `{ model_reasoning_summary }` into the thread config patch (same plumbing as `features.code_mode`).

## Tests

8 new tests in `thread-lifecycle.test.ts` covering each mapping, the unset/unknown passthrough cases, the no-clobber merge with other config keys, and the thread/resume parity path. All 35 tests in the file pass.

## Compatibility

- No schema change. `reasoningLevel` is already accepted by the `sessions.patch` schema; this only makes the codex extension honor it.
- Default behavior unchanged for sessions where `reasoningLevel` is unset ŌĆö codex's own default (and any user `~/.codex/config.toml`) still wins.
- No protocol or wire-format changes; `model_reasoning_summary` is a config key codex already understands


## PR-path real behavior proof ŌĆö controlled before/after on the same prompt

Captured live on an LXC running `openclaw 2026.5.7` / `@openclaw/codex 2026.5.7` / `@openai/codex 0.128.0`, model `gpt-5.5` via ChatGPT subscription OAuth. Token / email / hostname / full UUIDs redacted.

### Setup invariants (both runs)

```sh
$ cat ~/.openclaw/agents/coder/agent/codex-home/config.toml
[projects."/home/devuser/.openclaw/workspace-coder"]
trust_level = "trusted"
# (no model_reasoning_summary set ŌĆö the only thing that can produce summaries
#  is the extension's thread/start config)
```

- Same agent (`agent:coder:main`, `agentRuntime.id: "codex"`)
- Same prompt: `"What is 17 times 23? Briefly explain your reasoning."`
- Same `sessions.patch` payload: `{ thinkingLevel: "medium", reasoningLevel: "stream" }`
- Binding cleared and gateway restarted between runs so each takes the `thread/start` path

---

### BEFORE ŌĆö stock `@openclaw/codex 2026.5.7` bundle

Bundle confirmation:
```sh
$ grep -c 'resolveCodexReasoningSummary\|model_reasoning_summary\|reasoningSummary' \
    ~/.openclaw/npm/node_modules/@openclaw/codex/dist/thread-lifecycle-CzllX4PU.js
0
```

Live gateway WS run:
```
2026-05-13T13:01:33.540Z <- connect.challenge
2026-05-13T13:01:33.542Z -> connect (sent)
2026-05-13T13:01:33.574Z <- connect ok
2026-05-13T13:01:33.574Z -> sessions.patch { reasoningLevel: stream, thinkingLevel: medium }
2026-05-13T13:01:34.229Z <- sessions.patch ok
2026-05-13T13:01:34.229Z -> chat.send
2026-05-13T13:01:34.237Z <- chat.send ok runId=before-ŌĆ”
```

Session pref persisted (gateway state):
```json
{ "thinkingLevel": "medium", "reasoningLevel": "stream" }
```

Codex `turn_context` (in the rollout):
```json
{ "model": "gpt-5.5", "effort": "medium", "summary": "none" }
```

Rollout event-type counts:
```
   4  reasoning            (all 4 with "summary": [])
   1  turn_context
   1  agent_message
   0  summary_text
```

**Net effect**: `sessions.patch reasoningLevel: "stream"` is accepted and persisted on the openclaw side, but the codex extension never plumbs it into the codex `thread/start` config ŌĆö codex defaults `summary` to `"none"`, emits reasoning items with empty summary arrays, **zero `summary_text` events**, **zero `item/reasoning/summaryTextDelta` notifications** for `CodexAppServerEventProjector.handleReasoningDelta` to forward. Net silence to subscribers.

---

### AFTER ŌĆö bundle patched with this PR's logic

Bundle patched in place to apply the diff from this PR (helper + `buildThreadStartParams` config merge); a one-line `process.stderr.write("[pr-patch] reasoningLevel=ŌĆ” summary=ŌĆ” pid=ŌĆ”")` probe added inside `reasoningSummaryConfigPatch` to prove the helper fires at runtime.

```sh
$ grep -c 'resolveCodexReasoningSummary\|model_reasoning_summary' \
    ~/.openclaw/npm/node_modules/@openclaw/codex/dist/thread-lifecycle-CzllX4PU.js
3
$ node -c ~/.openclaw/npm/node_modules/@openclaw/codex/dist/thread-lifecycle-CzllX4PU.js && echo syntax OK
syntax OK
```

Live gateway WS run (same payload, same prompt):
```
2026-05-13T13:03:00.445Z <- connect.challenge
2026-05-13T13:03:00.447Z -> connect (sent)
2026-05-13T13:03:00.483Z <- connect ok
2026-05-13T13:03:00.484Z -> sessions.patch { reasoningLevel: stream, thinkingLevel: medium }
2026-05-13T13:03:01.040Z <- sessions.patch ok
2026-05-13T13:03:01.041Z -> chat.send
2026-05-13T13:03:01.049Z <- chat.send ok runId=after-ŌĆ”
```

Gateway journal ŌĆö PR helper probe fires with the session pref:
```
May 13 13:03:24 warm-ŌĆ” node[63576]: [pr-patch] reasoningLevel=stream summary=auto pid=63576
```

Codex `turn_context`:
```json
{ "model": "gpt-5.5", "effort": "medium", "summary": "auto" }
```

Rollout event-type counts:
```
   2  reasoning            (1 of 2 with non-empty summary)
   1  summary_text         <-- NEW
   1  turn_context
   1  agent_message
```

Captured `summary_text` content (excerpt, from the reasoning item):
```
**Checking for BOOTSTRAP**

I need to find out if BOOTSTRAP exists, especially since it seems like rg
isn't available. We didn't check with find yet, but maybe just checking if
BOOTSTRAP is there will be enough. I've noticed that the exec returned a
failure with rg. I might need to check the files using ls. This task seems
simple, but I must determine if BOOTSTRAP exists before proceeding with
any follow-up actions or deletions.
```

Final assistant answer (unchanged between runs):
```
17 ├Ś 23 = 391.

Reasoning: 17 ├Ś 20 = 340, and 17 ├Ś 3 = 51. Add them: 340 + 51 = 391.
```

---

### Side-by-side

| | session pref accepted | codex `turn_context.summary` | reasoning items / empty | `summary_text` events |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| BEFORE (stock bundle) | Ō£ō | `"none"` | 4 / 4 empty | **0** |
| AFTER (PR-patched bundle) | Ō£ō | `"auto"` | 2 / 1 empty | **1** |

The `item/reasoning/summaryTextDelta` stream this AFTER run produces is what `CodexAppServerEventProjector.handleReasoningDelta` (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.ts`, also visible in the installed bundle at `run-attempt-BaT_FcTv.js:1173`) already forwards as reasoning deltas to subscribed clients ŌĆö no projector change needed.

### Notes

- Probe + bundle patch were applied only for proof capture on this LXC; the PR source contains the same logic at `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts` (commits `6cec5562` + `8a7388dd`).
- Codex's `"auto"` mode is non-deterministic ŌĆö the model decides whether a given turn warrants emitting summary text. On trivial prompts it sometimes opts out (we observed this on a couple of math turns). `"auto"` was chosen to match the existing pi-embedded behavior (`src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts:1380: summary: options?.reasoningSummary || "auto"`). Forcing `"detailed"` in the patched bundle confirmed the path also produces summary text on those same trivial prompts.
- The follow-up commit (`8a7388dd`) responds to the previous review by dropping the `"off"` ŌåÆ `"none"` mapping, so the PR no longer clobbers a user's `~/.codex/config.toml` from openclaw's `"off"` default.


if you need more detailed logs, let me know. I can add.

The branch adds shell completion profile candidate resolution for zsh, fish, bash, and PowerShell, routes install/status checks and reload hints through resolver logic, and adds profile-path regression tests.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +236. Total +258 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main and v2026.6.9 hardcode zsh/fish profile paths under HOME and check bash `.bashrc` while install can write `.bash_profile`. I did not execute the shell install flow because this review is read-only.

target.title: fix(cli): honor ZDOTDIR/XDG_CONFIG_HOME in completion install (#63069)
target.body:
## Summary

Shell completion install/check helpers hardcoded zsh, fish, and bash profile paths under `$HOME`, ignoring `$ZDOTDIR` and `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` and disagreeing on bash `.bashrc` vs `.bash_profile`. Reporter on macOS / zsh with `$ZDOTDIR=$HOME/.config/zsh` saw completion silently written to `$HOME/.zshrc` (a file the shell never reads), so completion never activates in new shells. This PR centralizes profile-path resolution behind a single resolver that honors both env vars, treats `.bashrc`/`.bash_profile` as a priority-ordered candidate list, and lets the check and install paths agree on the same file.

## Root Cause

- `src/cli/completion-runtime.ts:129` (before): `getShellProfilePath()` returned `path.join(home, ".zshrc")`, `path.join(home, ".bashrc")`, `path.join(home, ".config", "fish", "config.fish")` regardless of `$ZDOTDIR` / `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME`.
- `src/cli/completion-runtime.ts:151` (before): `isCompletionInstalled()` called `getShellProfilePath(shell)` (HOME-only) and returned `false` for bash even when the install actually wrote to `.bash_profile`.
- `src/cli/completion-runtime.ts:215` (before): `installCompletion()` separately hardcoded `path.join(home, ".zshrc")`, `path.join(home, ".bashrc")` / `.bash_profile` fallback, and `path.join(home, ".config", "fish", "config.fish")` ŌĆö so when `.bashrc` was absent, install wrote to `.bash_profile` while check still looked at `.bashrc`, causing repeated `Enable completion?` prompts on every `openclaw update`.
- Execution path: `setup` / `update` / `completion --install` ŌåÆ `installCompletion()` writes to one path ŌåÆ `isCompletionInstalled()` (next session) checks the OTHER path ŌåÆ both report `false` so install is offered again, and the previously written source line lives in a file the shell never reads.
- clawsweeper review on #63069: *"Queue a focused fix PR because the report is valid, the affected files and tests are clear, and the target-repo implementation PR is closed unmerged."* and *"A shared resolver used by both status and install paths is the narrowest maintainable fix."*

## Changes

- `src/cli/completion-runtime.ts`: introduce `resolveShellProfilePathCandidates(shell, env, homeDir)` that returns candidate paths in priority order ŌĆö `$ZDOTDIR/.zshrc` then `$HOME/.zshrc` for zsh, `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/fish/config.fish` then `$HOME/.config/fish/config.fish` for fish, `.bashrc` then `.bash_profile` for bash. Make `getShellProfilePath()` async, return the first existing candidate (or the first candidate as the canonical install target when none exist). Update `isCompletionInstalled` and `usesSlowDynamicCompletion` to `await` it. Drop the duplicate hardcoded paths in `installCompletion` and route entirely through `getShellProfilePath`, so check and install always agree on the same file.
- `src/cli/completion-runtime.profile-path.test.ts` (new): 12 colocated regression tests covering `$ZDOTDIR` zsh priority, `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` fish priority, bash `.bashrc ŌåÆ .bash_profile` order, whitespace-only `$ZDOTDIR` handling, `os.homedir()` fallback when `env.HOME` is unset, and live `isCompletionInstalled()` against real temp directories where the OpenClaw marker lives in the non-default file.

Net diff: **+237 / ŌłÆ36 LOC across 2 files** (1 new test file + 1 production file).

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: macOS / zsh users with `$ZDOTDIR=$HOME/.config/zsh` (a common dotfile layout, often via `/etc/zshenv` or `.zshenv`) see `openclaw completion --install` write the source line to `$HOME/.zshrc` instead of `$ZDOTDIR/.zshrc`, so completion never loads in their shell. Same class of bug applies to fish users with `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` set, and to bash users who only have `~/.bash_profile` (macOS default): `isCompletionInstalled()` checks `.bashrc` while `installCompletion()` falls back to `.bash_profile`, so every `openclaw update` re-asks `Enable completion?`. Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/63069
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-65656` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/cli/completion-runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, slices the patched `resolveShellProfilePathCandidates` + `getShellProfilePath` block (offset-pinned), prints its SHA-256, then runs both functions against (a) synthetic env tables matching the reporter scenarios and (b) real temp directories on disk where the OpenClaw marker is dropped into the non-default file. No mocks. PR head: `5c935bf8` on `MoerAI:fix/shell-completion-xdg-zdotdir`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git checkout fix/shell-completion-xdg-zdotdir && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr63069.mts`. The smoke uses `fs.mkdtemp` to build a real `$ZDOTDIR/.zshrc` and `$HOME/.zshrc` pair, drops the `# OpenClaw Completion` marker into the `$ZDOTDIR` one only, and asks `getShellProfilePath("zsh", {HOME, ZDOTDIR})` which file it picks. Same for the macOS bash case where only `.bash_profile` exists.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `5c935bf8` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, patched resolver block 1961 bytes, SHA-256 `6d240bd88d09e8184ee26f26f4b7336d5a42940ecba6ce63e00e7fd65a31d3e6`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr63069.mts
=== Patched profile-path resolver ŌĆö bytes from src/cli/completion-runtime.ts ===
offset: 4838 -> 6799 ( 1961 bytes)
SHA-256: 6d240bd88d09e8184ee26f26f4b7336d5a42940ecba6ce63e00e7fd65a31d3e6

=== Scenario A: ZDOTDIR set, $ZDOTDIR/.zshrc preferred ===
candidates: [ '\\home\\example\\.config\\zsh\\.zshrc', '\\home\\example\\.zshrc' ]

=== Scenario B: ZDOTDIR unset, falls back to $HOME/.zshrc ===
candidates: [ '\\home\\example\\.zshrc' ]

=== Scenario C: bash .bashrc + .bash_profile (macOS fallback) ===
candidates: [ '\\home\\example\\.bashrc', '\\home\\example\\.bash_profile' ]

=== Scenario D: XDG_CONFIG_HOME set, fish preferred ===
candidates: [
  '\\home\\example\\.alt-config\\fish\\config.fish',
  '\\home\\example\\.config\\fish\\config.fish'
]

=== Scenario E: getShellProfilePath picks the first EXISTING candidate (ZDOTDIR wins) ===
zdotdir zshrc exists: true
home zshrc exists: true
getShellProfilePath returned: C:\Users\pss\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-pr63069-VIFFw8\.config\zsh\.zshrc
matches zdotdir candidate: true

=== Scenario F: macOS bash with only .bash_profile (no .bashrc) ===
.bashrc exists: false
.bash_profile exists: true
getShellProfilePath returned: C:\Users\pss\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-pr63069-bash-Aryipc\.bash_profile
matches .bash_profile: true

=== Pass/fail summary ===
  PASS  ZDOTDIR pref
  PASS  ZDOTDIR fallback
  PASS  no ZDOTDIR -> .zshrc
  PASS  bash .bashrc first
  PASS  bash .bash_profile 2nd
  PASS  fish XDG pref
  PASS  fish fallback
  PASS  picks existing zdotdir zshrc
  PASS  macOS picks .bash_profile w/o .bashrc
~~~

- Observed result after fix: The resolver returns `$ZDOTDIR/.zshrc` first when `ZDOTDIR` is set (Scenario A), `$HOME/.zshrc` when it is not (Scenario B), `.bashrc` then `.bash_profile` for bash (Scenario C), and `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/fish/config.fish` first when set (Scenario D). The live filesystem tests confirm `getShellProfilePath("zsh", {HOME, ZDOTDIR})` actually picks the existing `$ZDOTDIR` file when both candidates exist on disk (Scenario E, this directly satisfies the reporter's macOS / zsh / `$ZDOTDIR=$HOME/.config/zsh` setup), and falls through to `.bash_profile` on a macOS-style bash setup where only that file exists (Scenario F, this satisfies the bash `.bashrc`/`.bash_profile` check/install agreement bug). All 9 assertions PASS against the patched resolver pinned by SHA-256 `6d240bd88d09e8184ee26f26f4b7336d5a42940ecba6ce63e00e7fd65a31d3e6`.
- What was not tested: An interactive `openclaw onboard` / `openclaw update` flow was not exercised on the contributor machine. The fix is a pure path-resolution change inside the existing check/install helpers; the resolver returns plain strings that flow into `fs.readFile` / `fs.writeFile` unchanged, so the only behavioral change is the file path picked. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live macOS interactive smoke is required; the reporter's issue already contains a concrete `$ZDOTDIR=~/.config/zsh` reproducer.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/cli/completion-runtime.profile-path.test.ts` ŌĆö 12 new colocated regression cases (ZDOTDIR pref/fallback, whitespace-only ZDOTDIR, XDG fish pref/fallback, bash candidate order, os.homedir() fallback, plus 4 live-tempdir `isCompletionInstalled` cases covering ZDOTDIR priority, `.bash_profile`-only macOS, `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` fish, and the no-profile false case).
- `pnpm test src/cli/completion-cli.test.ts src/cli/completion-cli.write-state.test.ts src/wizard/setup.completion.test.ts` ŌĆö unchanged, exercise the broader completion CLI; the resolver refactor preserves the original behavior when neither `ZDOTDIR` nor `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set and `.bashrc` exists, so existing tests continue to pass.

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix for #63069. `installCompletion()` no longer duplicates the per-shell path logic; both the check path (`isCompletionInstalled`, `usesSlowDynamicCompletion`) and the install path now share `getShellProfilePath()`, so `.bashrc` vs `.bash_profile` and `$ZDOTDIR`/`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` stay consistent.
- Compatibility: when neither env var is set and `.bashrc` exists, the resolver returns exactly the same paths as the previous implementation, so existing installs are unaffected. Powershell paths are unchanged.
- Out of scope: `src/commands/doctor-completion.ts:111` and `:161` print hardcoded reload-hint strings (`source ~/.zshrc` etc.). Those are info-only messages on the success path and not on the failing `isCompletionInstalled` / `installCompletion` arithmetic; a separate cosmetic follow-up can route them through the same resolver.
- This follows the conservative direction in clawsweeper's review on #63069: *"A shared resolver used by both status and install paths is the narrowest maintainable fix; the closed target PR and cross-repo fork PR are useful source context, but the target repository still needs the change on main."*

Closes #63069

target.title: docs: clarify local-prefix managed Node runtime
target.body:
## Summary
- Clarify that `install-cli.sh` uses a managed local-prefix Node runtime that currently defaults to 22.22.0.
- Call out that this can intentionally differ from shell/global Node 24.
- Update install output wording so users see the managed-runtime distinction during install or update.

Closes #79558.

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `bash -n scripts/install-cli.sh`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm docs:check-links`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** The local-prefix installer now tells users that it is using a managed Node runtime, and that this runtime may differ from their shell/global Node.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux arm64 with Node v22.21.1, running the patched `scripts/install-cli.sh` from this PR branch.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
proof_tmp=$(mktemp -d)
mkdir -p "$proof_tmp/tools/node-v22.22.0/bin"
ln -s "$(command -v node)" "$proof_tmp/tools/node-v22.22.0/bin/node"
OPENCLAW_PREFIX="$proof_tmp" OPENCLAW_INSTALL_CLI_SH_NO_RUN=1 bash -c 'source scripts/install-cli.sh; install_node'
rm -rf "$proof_tmp"
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Console output from the patched installer helper:

```text
Using existing managed Node at /tmp/tmp.znUwhmDIh5/tools/node-v22.22.0; this local-prefix runtime may differ from your shell/global node.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The real installer path now surfaces the managed local-prefix Node distinction when reusing an existing managed Node, instead of silently skipping the Node step.
- **What was not tested:** I did not perform a full destructive/replacing install into a real `~/.openclaw` prefix; the proof uses a temporary local prefix to exercise the changed installer branch safely.

target.title: fix(amazon-bedrock-mantle): gate implicit discovery on AWS creds presence (#67288)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #67288. The `amazon-bedrock-mantle` plugin ran implicit Mantle provider discovery on every catalog refresh even when the user had no AWS credentials configured, emitting `[bedrock-mantle-discovery] Mantle IAM token generation unavailable` log lines on every request and attempting IAM bearer-token generation through `@aws/bedrock-token-generator` that was guaranteed to fail. The user-visible symptom is constant log noise on Raspberry Pi installs with no AWS env vars set.

The sibling `amazon-bedrock` plugin already has the gate: at `extensions/amazon-bedrock/discovery.ts:596`, implicit discovery short-circuits when `discovery.enabled !== true && !hasAwsCreds`. The mantle plugin had the `enabled === false` check but never the no-creds skip path.

## Fix

Mirror amazon-bedrock's gate in `resolveImplicitMantleProvider`:

```ts
const enabled = params.pluginConfig?.discovery?.enabled;
if (enabled === false) return null;
if (enabled !== true && !explicitBearerToken && !hasAwsSdkCreds(env)) return null;
```

Adds a small `hasAwsSdkCreds` helper that delegates to `resolveAwsSdkEnvVarName` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-auth-runtime` (same helper bedrock uses via `resolveBedrockConfigApiKey`). Also exports `MantleDiscoveryConfig` so the type is consumable from the registration site.

## Boundary

- Extension-local fix (`extensions/amazon-bedrock-mantle/`); no core or schema changes.
- No new config keys. Users who explicitly set `plugins.entries.amazon-bedrock-mantle.config.discovery.enabled: true` still get implicit discovery (and the IAM token generation attempt) even without AWS creds ŌĆö opt-in for cases where the bearer token is sourced through some out-of-band mechanism.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Users running OpenClaw on hosts with no AWS credentials (Raspberry Pi, default Docker installs) reported continuous `[bedrock-mantle-discovery] Mantle IAM token generation unavailable` log lines on every request. The mantle plugin's `resolveImplicitMantleProvider` would attempt `generateBearerTokenFromIam(...)` even when neither `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK` nor SDK creds were available, with the failed lookup logged on every catalog refresh. The amazon-bedrock plugin has a discovery.enabled gate plus a no-creds skip path; mantle was missing the no-creds skip.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at HEAD `995ed29231` on Node 22 / Darwin 25.2.0, exercising the production `resolveImplicitMantleProvider(...)` (`extensions/amazon-bedrock-mantle/discovery.ts`) against a real `process.env` shape (empty, with no AWS creds) and against the SDK-creds-aware `resolveAwsSdkEnvVarName` helper from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-auth-runtime`. No mocks of the resolver under test.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm test extensions/amazon-bedrock-mantle/discovery.test.ts` from the repository root, compiling the production source via tsgo and driving the live `resolveImplicitMantleProvider` against (a) empty env (no AWS creds) and (b) explicit `pluginConfig.discovery.enabled: false` with AWS bearer token set.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured terminal output from the `pnpm` invocation above on this exact branch:

  ```
   RUN  v4.1.6 /Users/shubh-trips/Documents/personal-project/open-source/openclaw

   Ō£ō |extension-providers| skips implicit discovery entirely when no AWS creds are present (#67288)
   Ō£ō |extension-providers| honors pluginConfig.discovery.enabled=false even when AWS creds are present (#67288)

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  29 passed (29)
     Duration  1.14s

  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.58s
  ```

  Runtime assertions captured by the live resolver runs: (a) with empty env (no `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK`, no SDK creds env), the resolver returns `null` AND the `tokenProviderFactory` spy is NOT called ŌĆö proving we short-circuit before IAM token generation; (b) with `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK` and `AWS_REGION` set but `pluginConfig.discovery.enabled: false`, the resolver still returns `null` and the token factory is not invoked ŌĆö proving the explicit-disable path wins over creds presence; (c) the 27 existing discovery tests all continue to pass, including the previously-passing "returns null when no auth is available" case (the old behavior path that the user complained about ŌĆö now it returns null *without* the noisy log).
- **Observed result after fix:** Installs without AWS credentials no longer see the per-request "Mantle IAM token generation unavailable" log lines and no longer attempt IAM bearer-token generation. The behavior for installs with `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK` set or with full SDK creds is unchanged. The behavior for explicit `discovery.enabled: false` is unchanged.
- **What was not tested:** End-to-end behavior on an actual ARM64 Raspberry Pi (the bug's reported environment) ŌĆö the regression test exercises the contract layer that the per-request catalog refresh consumes, which is what produced the user-visible log spam. Downstream catalog/registration flow is unchanged.

## Risk

- **Surface:** one bundled extension (`amazon-bedrock-mantle`), discovery-only gate.
- **Backwards compat:** installs that depend on implicit Mantle discovery without AWS creds env vars need to opt in via `plugins.entries.amazon-bedrock-mantle.config.discovery.enabled: true`. The original bug report explicitly disabled mantle to work around the noise, so the documented expected migration is: either set the bearer token / SDK creds, OR set `discovery.enabled: true` to opt in to the legacy noisy behavior.

## Closes

Fixes #67288

target.title: feat: add before_route_inbound_message plugin hook for channel bridging
target.body:
## Summary

Implements the `before_route_inbound_message` plugin hook as proposed in #81061.

This hook fires **before** OpenClaw resolves the canonical session key for an inbound message, allowing plugins (like the channel-bridge) to:

- **Redirect** messages to a different session via `redirectSessionKey`
- **Suppress** delivery entirely via `suppressDelivery`

## Hook Signature

```ts
type PluginHookBeforeRouteInboundMessageEvent = {
  channel: string;
  accountId?: string;
  conversationId?: string;   // NativeChannelId or OriginatingTo
  parentConversationId?: string;  // MessageThreadId (thread/topic ID)
  body: string;
  isGroup: boolean;           // derived from ChatType
  senderId?: string;          // From field
  originalSessionKey: string;
};

type PluginHookBeforeRouteInboundMessageResult = {
  handled: true;
  redirectSessionKey?: string;
  suppressDelivery?: boolean;
};
```

## Integration Points

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/plugins/hook-types.ts` | Added to `PluginHookName` union, `PLUGIN_HOOK_NAMES` array, and `PluginHookHandlerMap` |
| `src/plugins/hook-message.types.ts` | New event/context/result type definitions |
| `src/plugins/hooks.ts` | `runBeforeRouteInboundMessage()` using `runClaimingHook` |
| `src/channels/turn/kernel.ts` | Hook fired in `runChannelTurn()` after `resolveTurn()` |

## Pattern

Uses the **claiming hook** pattern (`runClaimingHook`) ŌĆö first handler returning `{ handled: true }` wins, matching `before_dispatch` and `reply_dispatch` semantics. This allows multiple plugins to register but only the highest-priority one takes effect.

## Why Here?

Hook fires in `runChannelTurn()` right after `resolveTurn()`, which is the convergence point for all channel adapters (Discord, Telegram, etc.). At this point:
- `ctxPayload.SessionKey` is already resolved and baked in
- The hook can update it before recording/dispatch downstream

This ensures redirects work across both modern adapters and compat paths.

## Unit Tests

Added 2 new tests in `src/channels/turn/kernel.test.ts`:

```
PASS   src/channels/turn/kernel.test.ts > channel turn kernel > redirects to a different session when before_route_inbound_message hook returns a redirect
PASS   src/channels/turn/kernel.test.ts > channel turn kernel > suppresses delivery when before_route_inbound_message hook returns suppressDelivery
```

Both tests verify:
- Hook fires with correct event context
- Redirect updates `ctxPayload.SessionKey` for downstream record/dispatch
- Suppression drops the message before recording

## Proof: supplied

This PR adds a new plugin hook infrastructure with unit test coverage. There is no in-tree plugin consumer yet, so runtime proof would require an external channel-bridge plugin implementation. The unit tests validate the hook fires correctly and that redirect/suppress behavior works as expected.

Fixes: #81061

target.title: [codex] Cron: repair empty sanitized final replies
target.body:
’╗┐???## Summary

- Problem: cron-owned agent runs could finish with no deliverable final text after sanitization and end up as silent `not-delivered` outcomes.
- Why it matters: scheduled reports can vanish without a clear error even though the cron job itself ran, which makes delivery failures hard to diagnose.
- What changed: add one repair pass for empty sanitized output, fail loudly when no deliverable text remains, enforce tool-disabled repair for embedded runs, and use an explicit CLI repair mode that resumes the first CLI session while disabling bundled MCP wiring.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does not relax reasoning-tag sanitization, does not change normal non-cron agent turns, and does not address the legacy local WhatsApp health-check cron issue tracked separately in #60204.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Exact bug issue: none existed at PR open time
- Related #60204 for the misleading legacy WhatsApp health-check path uncovered during the same investigation
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: cron delivery could receive an empty final text after sanitization (for example when malformed reasoning tags consumed the visible reply), but the cron runner had no recovery pass and the delivery path did not convert that condition into a hard error.
- Missing detection / guardrail: the runner did not retry with a constrained no-tools finalization prompt, and the direct-delivery path treated missing synthesized text too softly.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): the existing sanitization behavior is intentional; the gap was in cron finalization and delivery handling, not in the sanitizer itself.
- Why this regressed now: stricter sanitization can legitimately collapse malformed final replies to empty text, but cron still assumed a deliverable text reply would exist.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: ruled out transport-only failure for the investigated case; the actionable gap here was the empty-output cron path.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.interim-retry.test.ts`, `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.owner-auth.test.ts`, `src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: when cron final output becomes empty after sanitization, the runner performs one repair pass; embedded runs enforce `disableTools`, CLI runs resume the first CLI session and disable bundled MCP wiring, external-hook CLI cron runs keep `senderIsOwner=false` for both normal and repair calls, and if repair still yields no deliverable text the run fails loudly instead of silently ending `not-delivered`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug lives in cron run finalization plus delivery dispatch, and the narrowest stable guardrail is to exercise those exact seams with mocked agent output.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing interim-retry coverage already covered acknowledgement retries but not empty sanitized output.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Cron jobs that lose their visible final text after sanitization now get one repair attempt before delivery.
- If no deliverable text exists after that repair attempt, the cron run records an explicit error instead of silently finishing as `not-delivered`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[cron run] -> [sanitized final text becomes empty] -> [delivery path sees no text] -> [silent not-delivered]

After:
[cron run] -> [sanitized final text becomes empty] -> [no-tools repair pass]
          -> [recovered text -> deliver] or [still empty -> explicit cron error]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: the embedded repair path enforces `disableTools`; the CLI repair path is explicitly not represented as native-tool-free, resumes the first CLI session, and disables OpenClaw-bundled MCP wiring. The risk is limited to cron finalization behavior, and dedicated regression tests cover both embedded and CLI-backed paths.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu Linux
- Runtime/container: local Node + pnpm checkout
- Model/provider: mocked embedded + CLI runner paths in targeted tests
- Integration/channel (if any): cron + Telegram announce path in regression coverage
- Relevant config (redacted): default test fixtures

### Steps

1. Run `pnpm test -- src/cron/isolated-agent/run.interim-retry.test.ts`
2. Run `pnpm test -- src/cron/isolated-agent.skips-delivery-without-whatsapp-recipient-besteffortdeliver-true.test.ts`
3. Run `pnpm test -- src/agents/cli-runner.prepare.test.ts`

### Expected

- Empty sanitized cron output triggers one repair pass: embedded runs use `disableTools`, and CLI runs resume the first CLI session with bundled MCP disabled.
- If repair still produces no final text, the run finishes with an explicit error.
- CLI-backed repair mode does not inject bundled MCP overlays.

### Actual

- All three targeted tests pass with the new repair and fail-loud behavior in place.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: reviewed the cron finalization and delivery diff, ran the targeted regression tests locally, confirmed CLI repair resumes the first CLI session, confirmed external-hook CLI cron runs preserve non-owner sender scope for normal and repair calls, and confirmed the local changed-file check stack passed.
- Edge cases checked: empty payloads without descendants, repair success, repair failure, CLI repair-mode bundled MCP disablement, and first CLI session reuse during repair.
- What you did **not** verify: live WhatsApp/Telegram delivery against a real gateway session.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Cron-owned CLI agent runs that produce no deliverable final text now perform one repair pass and record the repaired final text instead of silently ending without a user-visible result. For CLI-backed cron runs, the repair pass resumes the first CLI session and disables OpenClaw-bundled MCP wiring without claiming native CLI tools are unavailable.

Real environment tested: Windows/PowerShell checkout. The live repair proof below was run at repair head `0f1b25fbfc`; current pushed head `d6ed463c96` keeps that repair path, additionally restores external-hook CLI non-owner sender scope, includes the upstream lint repair needed for the final CI head, and addresses the Greptile duplicate-descendant-predicate review note; verified with targeted red/green tests plus `pnpm lint --threads=8`. Local OpenClaw Gateway was at `ws://127.0.0.1:19162` with isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`; registered `codex-cli` runtime used installed OpenAI Codex CLI `codex-cli 0.130.0-alpha.5` through a thin proof wrapper around `codex exec --output-last-message`; cron delivery was disabled intentionally with `--no-deliver`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
corepack pnpm openclaw gateway health --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted> --json

corepack pnpm openclaw cron add --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted> --name "PR60212 fixed repair marker proof" --at "2030-01-08T00:00:00Z" --keep-after-run --session isolated --message "Real behavior proof for PR 60212. If the prompt you are reading contains the phrase Repair it now, reply exactly OPENCLAW_CRON_REPAIR_OK. Otherwise return an empty final answer and do not output any visible text." --no-deliver --timeout-seconds 300 --model openai/gpt-5.5 --json

corepack pnpm openclaw cron run 3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091 --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted> --expect-final --timeout 360000

corepack pnpm openclaw cron show 3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091 --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted> --json

corepack pnpm openclaw cron runs --id 3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091 --limit 5 --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted>
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
$ corepack pnpm openclaw gateway health --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted> --json
{
  "ok": true,
  "eventLoop": { "degraded": false, "reasons": [] },
  "plugins": { "loaded": ["acpx", "browser", "canvas", "device-pair", "file-transfer", "memory-core", "openai", "phone-control", "talk-voice"], "errors": [] }
}

$ corepack pnpm openclaw cron run 3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091 --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted> --expect-final --timeout 360000
{
  "ok": true,
  "enqueued": true,
  "runId": "manual:3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091:1778695539774:2"
}

$ corepack pnpm openclaw cron show 3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091 --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted> --json
{
  "id": "3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091",
  "name": "PR60212 fixed repair marker proof",
  "delivery": { "mode": "none", "channel": "last" },
  "state": {
    "lastRunStatus": "ok",
    "lastStatus": "ok",
    "lastDurationMs": 14308,
    "lastDeliveryStatus": "not-requested",
    "consecutiveErrors": 0
  }
}

$ corepack pnpm openclaw cron runs --id 3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091 --limit 5 --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted>
{
  "entries": [
    {
      "jobId": "3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091",
      "action": "finished",
      "status": "ok",
      "summary": "OPENCLAW_CRON_REPAIR_OK",
      "runId": "manual:3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091:1778695539774:2",
      "durationMs": 14308,
      "model": "gpt-5.5",
      "provider": "codex-cli",
      "delivered": false,

```

Observed result after fix: The real local gateway accepted the cron job, ran it through the registered `codex-cli` backend, recovered `OPENCLAW_CRON_REPAIR_OK` during the repair flow, and recorded the run as `status: "ok"`. Delivery was not requested by design because the proof command used `--no-deliver`; the current code keeps the CLI mode explicit, resumes the first CLI session for repair, and does not hit the earlier native-tool fail-closed error.

What was not tested: External WhatsApp/Telegram send was not exercised; delivery was disabled to keep the local proof isolated and non-invasive.

- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a cron run with intentionally empty final output now incurs one extra model pass before failing.
  - Mitigation: the retry is narrowly gated to empty, non-fatal, non-structured, no-descendant outcomes; embedded repair is tool-disabled and CLI repair is explicitly limited to bundled MCP suppression while resuming the first CLI session.
- Risk: CLI repair mode could accidentally preserve bundled MCP wiring.
  - Mitigation: `disableTools` now disables bundled MCP preparation, covered by `src/agents/cli-runner.prepare.test.ts`.

target.title: Redact exec tool result payloads
target.body:
## Summary
- redact exec foreground tool-result text and structured `details.aggregated` before returning results to the agent
- redact node-host exec stdout/stderr/error output before formatting tool results
- redact process poll/log/list output and structured details that can include output tails or aggregated process output

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Exec and background process tool results could return secret-shaped stdout/stderr/session text back to the agent. This patch redacts those returned tool-result surfaces before they leave the exec/process/node-host formatters.

**Real environment tested:** Real local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/exec-tool-result-redaction` at `da701a0bbf6c`, using an isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME`. The proof used an obvious fake canary value, not a real credential.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a local runtime proof that exercised `createExecTool` and `createProcessTool` with a fake `REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET` value, then inspected the returned tool-result `content[0].text` and structured `details.aggregated` values for foreground exec, process poll, process log, and process list tail/details surfaces.

**Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the local runtime proof:

```json
{
  "foregroundExec": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawswŌĆ”7890",
    "detailsAggregated": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawswŌĆ”7890",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processPoll": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawswŌĆ”7890\n\n\nProcess exited with code 0.",
    "detailsAggregated": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawswŌĆ”7890\n",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processLog": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawswŌĆ”7890",
    "detailsAggregated": "",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processList": {
    "status": "completed",
    "textTail": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawswŌĆ”7890",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  }
}
```

Supplemental focused regression coverage also exercises node-host exec stdout redaction by passing a fake node `system.run` payload through `executeNodeHostCommand` and asserting both returned text and `details.aggregated` redact the raw canary.

**Observed result after fix:** The raw fake canary value was absent from returned foreground exec, process poll, process log, and process list output. Redacted markers were present in the returned text/details where output was expected. The node-host formatter path is covered by the focused regression test for the same returned tool-result boundary.

**What was not tested:** Live node-host exec against a physical connected node was unavailable in this isolated proof environment; the node-host formatter was validated with the focused regression test that feeds a fake gateway node response through the production formatter path.

## Tests
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node-phases.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts`

- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped output'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node stdout'`

Notes: a full local run of the three targeted files is currently blocked in my environment by an unrelated invalid local `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` model alias (`openai-codex/gpt-5.3-codex`) during the pre-existing foreground timeout test. The new redaction regression tests pass individually under the agents-core Vitest config.

Fixes #71211



## Follow-up after review
- Added commit `bc79136bb7` to redact background exec `details.tail` before returning running-session tool results.
- Added regression coverage for background exec `details.tail`, process list command/tail details, and node-host stderr/error redaction in addition to stdout.

Additional local verification after follow-up:

Note: the full local three-file Vitest run still hits the pre-existing environment-sensitive approval path in `exec foreground failures > returns a failed text result when the default timeout is exceeded`, where local host exec approval intercepts `sleep 5` before the timeout assertion.

## Review feedback update - 2026-05-21

target.title: fix(agents): dedup subagent completion announces to prevent double Slack post
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds a process-local dedup/coalesce wrapper around `deliverSubagentAnnouncement`, keyed on the announce's stable `directIdempotencyKey`, so racing lifecycle/retry paths cannot post a subagent completion twice in the requester's Slack thread.
- Failed dispatches stay retryable (only `delivered: true` results are cached); concurrent callers for the same announce share a single in-flight dispatch.
- Adds an observability log line when the cache suppresses a duplicate dispatch.

## Root cause
Multiple lifecycle paths can invoke `runSubagentAnnounceFlow` ŌåÆ `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` for the same `(childSessionKey, childRunId)`: initial completion, deferred descendant-settle resume, pending-final retry, resume-on-restart. The existing safeguards (gateway `agent:${idempotencyKey}` cache with 5-minute TTL, registry `completionAnnouncedAt` guard, `beginSubagentCleanup` one-shot) can be bypassed by races or by the gateway dedup TTL rolling over. With no process-local dedup at the announce boundary itself, the same announce can be dispatched twice ŌĆö and each dispatch produces a user-facing post in the requester's channel.

## Fix
New `src/agents/subagent-announce-dedupe.ts` exports `runDedupedAnnounceDelivery(key, run)`:
- Namespaced keys (`deliverSubagentAnnouncement:<directIdempotencyKey>`) prevent any future cross-path collision with media-generate or orphan-recovery announces that use their own key shapes.
- 5-minute TTL matches the gateway dedup window.
- Only `delivered: true` results are cached; thrown errors and `delivered: false` outcomes release the in-flight slot so retries run fresh.
- Cache-hit and in-flight-coalesce events emit a `[subagent-announce] dedup ...` log line for ops visibility.

Wired into `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` (single change site) so all callers ŌĆö subagent lifecycle, orphan recovery, media-generate background tools ŌĆö get coverage.

## Test coverage
- `src/agents/subagent-announce-dedupe.test.ts` ŌĆö 7 unit tests: concurrent coalesce, repeat caching, failure not cached, distinct keys isolated, undefined key passthrough, thrown error releases in-flight slot, TTL=0 disables caching.
- `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` ŌĆö 4 new integration tests through the real `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` boundary:
  - Posts a Slack subagent completion only once when the same announce dispatches twice.
  - Retries delivery when the previous attempt did not produce a visible reply.
  - Retries delivery after a thrown gateway error.
  - Coalesces concurrent dispatches for the same announce key.
- `afterEach` in the delivery test now resets the new dedup cache to keep tests isolated.

## Does not affect
- Parent agent reporting paths (they do not go through `deliverSubagentAnnouncement`).
- Error reporting (`delivered: false` is not cached; errors propagate and retry).
- Progress updates (orphan-recovery uses distinct `<runId>:recovery-progress` keys per attempt, intentionally non-colliding).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: subagent task completion message is posted twice in the same Slack thread when `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` is invoked twice for the same `(childSessionKey, childRunId)` (initial complete + retry/resume path running close together, or the gateway dedup TTL rolling over between attempts).

**Real environment tested**: local OpenClaw runtime on macOS (Darwin 25.4.0, Node 24.15.0), production source loaded under `node --import tsx` ŌĆö same module graph the gateway uses at runtime. Only the *outbound gateway transport* is stubbed so the test can count dispatch attempts; the dedup wrapper, dispatch router, and origin resolution all run from production source.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
node --import tsx /tmp/announce-dedupe-proof.mjs        # after fix: dispatches twice via deliverSubagentAnnouncement
node --import tsx /tmp/announce-dedupe-proof-before.mjs # baseline: dispatches twice via the raw runSubagentAnnounceDispatch the patch wraps
```
Both repro scripts construct a realistic Slack-channel `directIdempotencyKey` (`announce:v1:agent:worker:subagent:child:run-live-dedup`) and a thread-bound delivery context (`channel: slack`, `to: channel:C123`, `threadId: 171.222`) and call the production module twice with identical params.

**Evidence after fix**: live console capture from `node --import tsx /tmp/announce-dedupe-proof.mjs`, showing the new dedup log line firing on the second dispatch and the stubbed gateway dispatch counter stuck at 1:
```
[repro] dispatching announce #1
[repro] #1 result: {"delivered":true,"path":"direct","phases":[{"phase":"direct-primary","delivered":true,"path":"direct"}]}
[repro] gateway dispatches after #1: 1
[repro] dispatching announce #2 (same key)
[subagent-announce] dedup cache hit for announce:v1:agent:worker:subagent:child:run-live-dedup; suppressed duplicate dispatch (path=direct)
[repro] #2 result: {"delivered":true,"path":"direct","phases":[{"phase":"direct-primary","delivered":true,"path":"direct"}]}
[repro] gateway dispatches after #2: 1
[repro] second result is same object as first: true
```

Before-fix comparison ŌĆö same scenario but bypassing the new wrapper by driving `runSubagentAnnounceDispatch` directly (this is what `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` did before the patch):
```
[repro-before] dispatching announce #1 (no dedup)
[repro-before] #1 result: {"delivered":true,"path":"direct","phases":[{"phase":"direct-primary","delivered":true,"path":"direct"}]}
[repro-before] gateway dispatches after #1: 1
[repro-before] dispatching announce #2 (same key, no dedup)
[repro-before] #2 result: {"delivered":true,"path":"direct","phases":[{"phase":"direct-primary","delivered":true,"path":"direct"}]}
[repro-before] gateway dispatches after #2: 2
[repro-before] second result is same object as first: false
```

**Observed result after fix**: with the dedup wrapper in place, a second invocation of `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` for the same announce key returns the cached `delivered: true` result without re-dispatching to the gateway. Gateway dispatch counter stays at 1 across both calls; the second caller receives the same result object as the first (identity equal); the new `[subagent-announce] dedup cache hit ...` log line is emitted with the announce key and the original delivery path. The unpatched comparison shows gateway dispatches = 2 and two distinct result objects ŌĆö i.e., what produced the duplicate Slack post in production.

**What was not tested**: live Slack workspace round-trip with a real bot token (I do not have a Slack workspace + OpenClaw gateway pair available here). The fix lives entirely above the channel layer ŌĆö once `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` is suppressed, no Slack `chat.postMessage` is issued ŌĆö so the live Slack post count is bounded by the number of times the gateway is dispatched, which the captures above show is now exactly 1.

## Verification
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-dedupe.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` ŌĆö 44/44 passing.
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce.timeout.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-dispatch.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-queue.test.ts src/agents/subagent-orphan-recovery.test.ts` ŌĆö 70/70 passing.
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.announce-loop-guard.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.steer-restart.test.ts` ŌĆö 53/53 passing.
- `pnpm check:changed` ŌĆö green (typecheck core + tests, lint core, runtime cycle and sidecar guards).
- Independent code review via the `code-reviewer` agent: safe-to-ship; namespace key + thrown-error retry test addressed.

## Test plan
- [ ] Spawn a subagent task in a Slack thread; confirm exactly one completion message lands and `[subagent-announce] dedup cache hit ...` appears in logs only on duplicated lifecycle paths.
- [ ] Force a transient announce delivery error (e.g., disconnect the gateway briefly mid-announce); confirm the eventual retry still produces exactly one final completion post and no dedup log fires for the failed first attempt.
- [ ] Cron-driven completion announces (different code path through the same `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` boundary) continue to deliver once.

target.title: fix(sessions): migrate session naming to canonical title
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Session naming was partly migrated from legacy `label` to canonical user-editable `title`, but the runtime contract was incomplete. New gateway schemas accepted `title` while `sessions.create` dropped it, `sessions.list` did not project or filter it, and the durable `SessionEntry` type did not declare the persisted field.

Additionally, two regressions existed in the patch path:

1. `sessions.patch({ label: null })` and `sessions.patch({ title: null })` crashed because `normalizeSessionTitleText(null)` calls `null.trim()` before the null-clear branch.
2. Mixed null/string pairs like `{ title: null, label: "foo" }` were silently accepted ŌĆö the null won and cleared both fields, ignoring the value the client intended to set.

## Why This Change Was Made

This completes the title contract while preserving the legacy label mirror:

- `sessions.create` now passes `title` into the shared patch applier, which mirrors it to `label` for compatibility.
- `SessionEntry` now declares `title?: string` as durable session metadata.
- Gateway session rows now project normalized `title` from `title` or legacy `label`.
- `sessions.list({ title })` now filters by the same normalized title comparison used by patch and resolve.
- Nullable `title: null` and `label: null` clears are preserved as sentinels before string normalization.
- Mixed null/string title+label patch pairs are explicitly rejected as ambiguous.
- `/name --clear` documented in slash-commands.md.
- Focused tests and proof script cover create, patch, resolve, list projection, legacy compat, nullable clears, and mixed-pair rejections.

## User Impact

Users and clients can set, clear, list, create, patch, and resolve session names through the canonical `title` field without breaking existing `label` consumers. `/name --clear` removes the session name. Legacy label-only entries still appear as titled rows and remain resolvable through title-based lookup.

## Evidence

Head SHA: `e988d5d5bf`

### Real behavior proof: session title contract end-to-end

Ran `scripts/proof-session-title.ts` against the patched modules ŌĆö exercises `sessions-patch.ts`, `session-label.ts`, and `session-utils.ts` with real session entries (no mocks, no Telegram).

```
=== Proof 1: parseSessionTitle / parseSessionLabel ===
  PASS  parseSessionTitle("My Session") succeeds
  PASS  title value preserved
  PASS  title is trimmed
  PASS  parseSessionTitle("") fails
  PASS  parseSessionTitle(null) fails
  PASS  parseSessionTitle(123) fails
  PASS  parseSessionLabel("Legacy Label") succeeds
  PASS  label value preserved
  PASS  parseSessionLabel("") fails

=== Proof 2: applySessionTitle ŌĆö set and clear ===
  PASS  set: title stored
  PASS  set: label mirror populated
  PASS  clear: title removed
  PASS  clear: label mirror removed

=== Proof 3: getSessionTitleFromEntry ŌĆö priority ===
  PASS  title wins over label
  PASS  label used when no title
  PASS  empty entry ŌåÆ undefined
  PASS  null entry ŌåÆ undefined

=== Proof 4: sessionTitlesEqual ===
  PASS  NFC-equal strings match
  PASS  different strings don't match
  PASS  null left ŌåÆ false
  PASS  undefined right ŌåÆ false

=== Proof 5: sessions.create ŌĆö set title on new session ===
  PASS  create with title succeeds
  PASS  title persisted
  PASS  label mirror set

=== Proof 6: /name <title> ŌĆö rename session ===
  PASS  rename succeeds
  PASS  title updated
  PASS  label mirror updated

=== Proof 7: /name --clear ŌĆö clear via title: null ===
  PASS  clear via title: null succeeds
  PASS  title cleared
  PASS  label mirror cleared

=== Proof 8: legacy label: null clear ===
  PASS  clear via label: null succeeds
  PASS  title cleared
  PASS  label cleared

=== Proof 9: both title: null + label: null ===
  PASS  both-null clear succeeds
  PASS  title cleared
  PASS  label cleared

=== Proof 10: reject mixed null/string ===
  PASS  title: null + label: string ŌåÆ rejected
  PASS  error message mentions mixed clear/set
  PASS  title: string + label: null ŌåÆ rejected
  PASS  error message mentions mixed clear/set

=== Proof 11: label-only set (backward compat) ===
  PASS  label-only set succeeds
  PASS  label set

=== Proof 12: title/label mismatch rejection ===
  PASS  title: Alpha + label: Beta ŌåÆ rejected
  PASS  error mentions mismatch

=== Proof 13: sessions.list ŌĆö title projection (label-only entry) ===
  PASS  label-only entry projects title from label

=== Proof 14: sessions.resolve ŌĆö title lookup matches label-only entry ===
  PASS  title lookup matches label-only entry

=== Proof 15: full round-trip ŌĆö create ŌåÆ rename ŌåÆ clear ŌåÆ legacy lookup ===
  PASS  round-trip: create succeeds
  PASS  round-trip: title after create
  PASS  round-trip: rename succeeds
  PASS  round-trip: title after rename
  PASS  round-trip: label after rename
  PASS  round-trip: clear succeeds
  PASS  round-trip: title after clear
  PASS  round-trip: label after clear
  PASS  round-trip: legacy label-only resolves as title

  Results: 55 passed, 0 failed
```

### Vitest: all touched tests pass on current head

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-name.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.create.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.test.ts
```

```
Gateway shard:  Test Files 14 passed (14), Tests 734 passed (734)
Auto-reply shard:  Test Files 1 passed (1), Tests 7 passed (7)
Total: 741 passed in 133.68s
```

### Regression fix 1: nullable clear sentinels

**Bug:** `sessions-patch.ts:362-363` called `normalizeSessionTitleText()` on patch values whenever they were not `undefined`, including `null`. Caused `null.trim()` to throw.

**Fix:** Check for `null` explicitly. `null` passes through as a sentinel; string values still normalized with `trim().normalize("NFC")`.

### Regression fix 2: mixed null/string title+label pairs

**Bug:** `{ title: null, label: "foo" }` silently cleared both fields, ignoring the set value.

**Fix:** Explicitly reject when one field is `null` and the other is a non-null string:

```typescript
if (
  (rawTitle === null && rawLabel !== undefined && rawLabel !== null) ||
  (rawLabel === null && rawTitle !== undefined && rawTitle !== null)
) {
  return invalid("cannot clear title or label while setting the other");
}
```

### Docs

`docs/tools/slash-commands.md` updated: `/name` description now documents `/name --clear`.

### Lint gate

`check-lint` passes on current head.

### ClawSweeper P2 findings addressed (from previous review)

1. **sessions-patch.ts: compare title/label after parsing** ŌĆö Normalized with `trim().normalize("NFC")` before comparison.
2. **session-display.ts: add title to shared UI row contract** ŌĆö `GatewaySessionRow` in `ui/src/ui/types.ts` now declares `title?: string`.

### Mantis live proof

`@openclaw-mantis` has been asked to capture Telegram live proof (see PR comments). The proof script above validates the same code paths end-to-end.

target.title: feat(context-engine): add interceptCompaction contract for context-engine plugins
target.body:
TLDR - lets other context systems turn off/replace codex compaction (when it calls to compact it uses them versus its native).

## Summary

Adds an optional `interceptCompaction(request)` method to the `ContextEngine` plugin contract so engines can replace the runtime's default `session_before_compact` GPT-driven summarization with their own assembly ŌĆö without leaving the runtime's compaction lifecycle.

Motivating use case: [lossless-claw](https://github.com/Martian-Engineering/lossless-claw/pull/665) assembles a lossless raw + summary pyramid on disk and wants to swap in its assembled context whenever pi-coding-agent would otherwise trigger codex's GPT compaction. With the contract added here, lossless-claw can keep its plugin scope narrow (one new method on the engine) instead of forking or monkey-patching the runtime.

## Architecture ŌĆö two compaction lanes, two capability flags

OpenClaw + pi-coding-agent expose two distinct compaction flows that this PR makes explicit:

```
ŌöīŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöÉ

ŌööŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöś

ŌöīŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöÉ
```
