target.title: fix(lmstudio): bound model load success response body to prevent OOM
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`ensureLmstudioModelLoaded` in `extensions/lmstudio/src/models.fetch.ts` POSTs to LM Studio's `/api/v1/models/load` and reads the **success** response with an unbounded `await response.json()`. LM Studio is a self-hosted/local server endpoint that the agent reaches over HTTP; a misbehaving, misconfigured, or compromised server (or anything sitting on the configured `baseUrl`, including an SSRF-redirected target) can return a success body with no `Content-Length` and stream an arbitrarily large JSON payload.

Because `response.json()` buffers the entire body before parsing, that unbounded body is fully read into memory on the provider/discovery path before any size check runs. That is a memory-pressure / hang vector on a code path that runs during model discovery and before first inference ŌĆö exactly the kind of unbounded external-response read the response-limit campaign has been closing across the codebase.

The sibling **error** body on the same endpoint was already bounded (`readResponseTextLimited`, 8 KiB) in a prior change. This PR closes the remaining unbounded read: the **success** JSON body.

## Changes

- `extensions/lmstudio/src/models.fetch.ts`: replace the unbounded `await response.json()` on the `/api/v1/models/load` success path with the shared, already-bounded `readProviderJsonResponse<LmstudioLoadResponse>(...)` helper (re-exported from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-http`, same helper the `/api/v1/models` discovery path already uses). It reads the body through `readResponseWithLimit` under the 16 MiB provider-JSON cap, cancels the stream on overflow, and wraps malformed JSON with the caller's label. No new abstraction is introduced and the redundant local `try/catch` is removed.
- `extensions/lmstudio/src/models.test.ts`: migrate the model fetch/load mocks from bare `{ ok, json }` placeholders to real `Response` objects (the bounded readers require a real body stream), and add a regression test that streams an oversized (>16 MiB) success body and asserts the load path throws a bounded error and cancels the stream instead of buffering it all. Updates the malformed-load assertion to the helper's owned message.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: unbounded buffering of the LM Studio `/api/v1/models/load` success JSON body; after the fix the read is capped at 16 MiB and the stream is cancelled on overflow.
- **Real environment tested**: a local `node:http` server (real sockets, chunked transfer-encoding, no `Content-Length`) driving the real exported `ensureLmstudioModelLoaded` / `readProviderJsonResponse` via the global `fetch` (real `Response` body streams). Run with `node --import tsx proof.mts`.
- **Exact steps**: (1) normal small `{ "status": "loaded" }` response, end-to-end `ensureLmstudioModelLoaded`; (2) oversized 20 MiB chunked success body, end-to-end; (3) a body that hangs forever after a prefix, driving `readProviderJsonResponse` with an explicit small cap; (4) negative control reading the same 20 MiB body with the pre-fix unbounded `await response.text()`.
- **Evidence after fix**: (1) returns `"proof-model"` (still parses normally); (2) throws `LM Studio model load: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes` and the server observed only ~17.5 MiB sent before the socket closed (vs 20 MiB total) ŌĆö the stream was cancelled mid-flight; (3) throws `... exceeds 2048 bytes` and the server socket was closed (cancelled) instead of hanging; (4) the unbounded read consumed the full 20 MiB, confirming the cap is load-bearing.
- **Observed result**: oversized/hanging bodies are stopped at the cap with a bounded error and the upstream stream is cancelled; well-formed small bodies still parse.
- **What was not tested**: a real third-party LM Studio binary returning a pathological body (used a faithful local HTTP server instead); behavior under genuine OS memory exhaustion (the goal is to prevent reaching it).

## Evidence

```
[proof] µ£¼Õ£░ server ÕÉ»ÕŖ© http://127.0.0.1:36821
PASS  normal-small ń½»Õł░ń½»Ķ¦Żµ×ÉõĖŹńĀ┤ ŌĆö ensureLmstudioModelLoaded Ķ┐öÕø× proof-model
PASS  oversized µŖø bounded error ŌĆö error="LM Studio model load: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes"
PASS  oversized server ÕÅæķĆüķćÅ < Õģ©ķćÅ 20MiB(stream Ķó½µÅÉÕēŹ cancel) ŌĆö server Õ«×ÕÅæ 17.50MiB (cap 16MiB,Ķ┐£Õ░Åõ║Ä 20MiB Õģ©ķćÅ)
PASS  hang body Õł░ cap ÕŹ│µŖø bounded error(õĖŹµŚĀķÖÉµīéĶĄĘ) ŌĆö error="LM Studio model load: JSON response exceeds 2048 bytes"
PASS  hang body cap ÕæĮõĖŁÕÉÄ server ń½» socket Ķó½ cancel/close ŌĆö server Õ«×ÕÅæ 720922 bytes ÕÉÄ socket close=true
PASS  negative control:µŚĀńĢī await .text() Õģ©ķćÅĶ»╗Õģź 20MiB(Ķ»üµśÄ cap load-bearing) ŌĆö µŚĀńĢīĶ»╗Õł░ 20.00MiB µ¢ćµ£¼ / server Õ«×ÕÅæ 20.00MiB(Õ»╣ńģ¦:bounded õ╗ģĶ»╗Õł░ cap)
[proof] ALL PASS
```

Unit tests: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/lmstudio/src/models.test.ts --run` ŌåÆ 24 passed (24), including the new `bounds oversized model load success bodies`. `oxlint` and `tsgo` (extensions source + test) clean on the changed files.

**Label**: security

---

This is the symmetric companion to the response-limit campaign (e.g. #95103 / #95108 / #95218): the LM Studio discovery `/api/v1/models` path and the `/api/v1/models/load` error body were already bounded; this PR finishes the set by bounding the `/api/v1/models/load` success body with the same shared helper and cap.

AI-assisted: implemented and proof-tested with AI assistance; reviewed by a human before submission.

target.title: fix(ollama): bound model-discovery JSON response reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`extensions/ollama/src/provider-models.ts` discovers Ollama models by calling `/api/tags`
(`fetchOllamaModels`) and `/api/show` (`queryOllamaModelShowInfo`), and parsed both responses with
an **unbounded** `await response.json()`. The Ollama base URL is user-supplied and can point at a
remote/cloud endpoint (`https://ollama.com`, a self-hosted box, or a host reachable via SSRF), so
these discovery responses are untrusted input. A hostile or buggy server can return a
`Content-Length`-less JSON body that streams forever (or many MiB), and `response.json()` buffers the
entire payload before parsing ŌĆö driving the provider model-discovery path into memory pressure / OOM
or hanging the discovery call. This is not a cosmetic cleanup: the read sits on the provider
onboarding/refresh path that runs against an attacker-influenceable endpoint.

## Changes

- Add a `OLLAMA_DISCOVERY_JSON_MAX_BYTES` cap (16 MiB, aligned with the merged provider-JSON
  bound-stream PRs #95218 / #95418 / #95420) and a small `readOllamaDiscoveryJson<T>(response, label)`
  helper that reads the success body via `readResponseWithLimit` from `@openclaw/media-core`
  (re-exported through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/response-limit-runtime`, the same path style this module
  already uses for `ssrf-runtime`), cancelling the stream on overflow, then `JSON.parse`s the decoded
  bytes. No new abstraction ŌĆö it reuses the shared bounded reader.
- Route both `/api/tags` (`fetchOllamaModels`) and `/api/show` (`queryOllamaModelShowInfo`) through
  the bounded reader instead of `await response.json()`.
- **Fail-soft semantics preserved**: the overflow throw is caught by the existing discovery error
  handling, so a capped endpoint degrades gracefully ŌĆö `/api/tags` returns
  `{ reachable: false, models: [] }` and `/api/show` returns `{}`. No OOM, no hang.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Unbounded `await response.json()` on the two Ollama discovery reads lets an
  untrusted endpoint stream an unbounded JSON body into memory.
- **Real environment tested**: A real local `node:http` server (`createServer`) bound to
  `127.0.0.1`, returning a `Content-Length`-less body that streams 64 KiB chunks until the socket is
  torn down (would total ~64 MiB, 4├Ś the 16 MiB cap). The **real exported** `fetchOllamaModels` and
  `queryOllamaModelShowInfo` were driven against it (through the real `fetchWithSsrFGuard` dispatcher,
  with the loopback allowed by the existing `buildOllamaBaseUrlSsrFPolicy`).
- **Exact steps**: `node --import tsx proof.mts` ŌĆö start server ŌåÆ drive the real functions ŌåÆ
  read back fail-soft results + the server's observed bytes-sent / socket-abort counters ŌåÆ run a
  negative control (raw unbounded read of the same endpoint) ŌåÆ flip the server to small valid bodies
  and re-drive the real functions.
- **Evidence after fix**: `readResponseWithLimit` threw `... exceeds 16777216 bytes`; the server
  observed the socket aborted after ~17ŌĆō19 MiB (Ōē¬ the ~64 MiB it would have sent), confirming the
  stream was **cancelled**, not drained. `fetchOllamaModels` returned `{ reachable: false, models: [] }`
  and `queryOllamaModelShowInfo` returned `{}` (fail-soft intact).
- **Observed result (all 8 checks PASS)**:
  - `readResponseWithLimit throws bounded error on unbounded body` ŌåÆ threw, msg `exceeds 16777216 bytes`
  - `stream cancelled: server saw socket abort` ŌåÆ aborted=true, bytesSent=19333120 (< 33554432)
  - `fetchOllamaModels fail-soft on overflow` ŌåÆ `{ reachable:false, models:[] }`
  - `queryOllamaModelShowInfo fail-soft on overflow` ŌåÆ `{}`
  - `show stream cancelled` ŌåÆ aborted=true, bytesSent=17825792
  - `negative control: unbounded read buffers PAST the 16 MiB cap` ŌåÆ buffered 16799524 bytes (> cap)
    ŌĆö proves the cap is load-bearing (without it the body keeps accumulating past 16 MiB).
  - `happy path: small /api/tags still parsed` ŌåÆ `reachable:true, 2 models`
  - `happy path: small /api/show still parsed` ŌåÆ `contextWindow=65536`
- **What was not tested**: Did not exercise a live external Ollama Cloud endpoint (`ollama.com`); the
  untrusted-body behavior is fully reproduced with a local streaming server, which is the same
  transport path. The proof script is not committed.

## Evidence

```
[proof] local server on http://127.0.0.1:46829, cap=16777216 bytes, would-streamŌēł67108864 bytes

PASS  readResponseWithLimit throws bounded error on unbounded body :: threw=true msg="bounded: exceeds 16777216 bytes"
PASS  stream cancelled: server saw socket abort before sending the full ~64MiB :: aborted=true bytesSent=19333120 (<33554432)
PASS  fetchOllamaModels fail-soft on overflow (reachable:false, models:[]) :: {"reachable":false,"models":[]}
PASS  queryOllamaModelShowInfo fail-soft on overflow ({}) :: {}
PASS  show stream cancelled: server saw socket abort, bytes Ōē¬ full body :: aborted=true bytesSent=17825792
PASS  negative control: unbounded read buffers PAST the 16MiB cap (cap is load-bearing) :: buffered=16799524 bytes (>16777216)
PASS  happy path: small /api/tags still parsed (2 models) :: {"reachable":true,"models":[{"name":"llama3:8b"},{"name":"qwen3:4b"}]}
PASS  happy path: small /api/show still parsed (contextWindow=65536) :: contextWindow=65536

[proof] ALL PASS
```

Regression suite + checks (worktree on `upstream/main`):
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/ollama/src/provider-models.test.ts --run` ŌåÆ **16/16 passed**
- `oxlint extensions/ollama/src/provider-models.ts` ŌåÆ exit 0, clean
- `tsgo -p tsconfig.extensions.json` ŌåÆ no errors in `extensions/ollama` (only pre-existing,
  unrelated `extensions/qa-lab` `crabline` module-resolution failures present on untouched main)

This is the symmetric counterpart to the #95103 / #95108 response-limit campaign, applying the same
`@openclaw/media-core` bounded reader to the two Ollama provider-discovery JSON reads.

**Label**: security

_AI-assisted._

commands.author: Alix-007

target.title: fix(mattermost): bound successful REST JSON/text response reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/client.ts` talks to a Mattermost server that the plugin does not control ŌĆö self-hosted instances, LAN deployments, or a host that has been compromised or is malfunctioning. The client already hardened two of its three read paths:

- error bodies are bounded via `readResponseTextLimited` (8 KiB), and
- guarded responses are streamed through `responseWithRelease` instead of `arrayBuffer()`,

but the **successful** response path was still unbounded. `request()` called `await res.json()` / `await res.text()`, and `uploadMattermostFile()` called `await res.json()` on the file-info result. Each of those buffers the entire body into memory before parsing.

A Mattermost server can return a response with **no `Content-Length`** and an arbitrarily large ŌĆö or never-terminating ŌĆö JSON/text body (every REST call goes through `request()`: users, channels, posts, file uploads). Because nothing capped the success path, the plugin would buffer the whole stream, applying unbounded memory pressure / hang to the plugin and the surrounding agent runtime. This is not generic cleanup: it closes the one remaining unbounded ingest point on an untrusted-server boundary.

## Changes

- `request()` success path: parse JSON via the shared `readProviderJsonResponse` (16 MiB cap; cancels the stream and throws a bounded `ŌĆ” exceeds 16777216 bytes` error on overflow, then wraps malformed JSON), matching the provider HTTP path used elsewhere in the repo.
- `request()` non-JSON success path: read via `readResponseTextLimited` with a 64 KiB cap (non-JSON success bodies are a rare fallback; the API is JSON-first).
- `uploadMattermostFile()` file-info JSON: bounded the same way through `readProviderJsonResponse`.
- Reuses existing helpers only (`readProviderJsonResponse`, `readResponseTextLimited` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-http`) ŌĆö no new abstraction, and no second buffering layer added to the guarded fetch adapter or the error path.
- Added a regression test: oversized guarded success JSON throws the bounded error and cancels the upstream stream early.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: a successful Mattermost REST JSON/text response with no `Content-Length` and a body larger than the cap is no longer buffered unbounded; the stream is cancelled at the cap and a bounded error is thrown. Normal small responses still parse intact.
- **Real environment tested**: local `node:http` server bound to `127.0.0.1` (no `Content-Length`), driving the **real exported** `createMattermostClient(...).request(...)` and `uploadMattermostFile(...)` through the production guarded fetch path (`fetchWithSsrFGuard` + `allowPrivateNetwork`), under `node --import tsx`.
- **Exact steps**:
  1. Server streams 1 MiB `{`-chunks forever with `content-type: application/json` and **no** `Content-Length`.
  2. `client.request("/flood-json")` is awaited; the server counts chunks actually written and tracks socket close.
  3. `uploadMattermostFile(...)` is pointed at the same flood endpoint.
  4. Negative control: a finite-but-oversized 40 MiB body is read once via the **old** unbounded `await res.text()` and once via the new bounded `request()`.
  5. Normal small JSON and small text responses are requested to confirm they still parse intact.
- **Evidence after fix** (proof harness, 7/7 PASS):
  ```
  PASS normal-json-intact {"id":"post-1","message":"hello"}
  PASS normal-text-intact "plain-ok-body"
  PASS oversized-json-throws-bounded Mattermost API /flood-json: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes
  PASS oversized-json-cancelled-early chunksWrittenAtThrow=19
  PASS oversized-json-socket-released socketClosed=true
  PASS upload-oversized-throws-bounded Mattermost API /files: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes
  PASS negative-control-unbounded-buffers-all unboundedBytes=42991618 serverWroteMiB=41 (bounded path cancelled at 19 MiB)
  SUMMARY pass=7 fail=0
  ```
- **Observed result**: bounded path throws `JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes`, the server stops after writing ~17-19 MiB (reader cancelled just past the 16 MiB cap), and the socket is released. The negative control proves the cap is load-bearing: the **old** `await res.text()` drained and buffered the entire 41 MiB body (42,991,618 bytes) ŌĆö i.e. it would have OOM'd on a truly unbounded body ŌĆö while the new path cancelled at ~19 MiB.
- **What was not tested**: I did not run against a real production Mattermost server (used a local untrusted-server simulator), and did not change the websocket / media binary download paths or the `readMattermostError` `!res.body` in-memory fallback branch (already bounded / not an unbounded network read).

## Evidence

- Proof harness: `node --import ./node_modules/tsx/dist/loader.mjs proof.mts` ŌåÆ `SUMMARY pass=7 fail=0` (output above; harness not committed).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/client.test.ts --run` ŌåÆ **23 passed** (includes the new "bounds and cancels oversized guarded Mattermost success JSON bodies" regression test).
- `oxlint extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/client.ts` ŌåÆ clean.
- `tsgo:extensions` (`tsconfig.extensions.json`, the CI extensions typecheck) ŌåÆ no `mattermost/client.ts` errors.
- `check-extension-plugin-sdk-boundary.mjs --mode=plugin-sdk-internal` ŌåÆ no violations (imports are public `plugin-sdk` entrypoints).

This is the symmetric follow-up to the #95103 / #95108 response-limit campaign, applying the same bounded-stream discipline to the last unbounded read point in the Mattermost REST client's success path.

**Label**: security

AI-assisted.

commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" ŌĆö users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened on **both** the ACP dispatch path and the non-ACP dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`).

This PR eliminates the churn on **both paths** by introducing a `captionedFinalText` channel capability, suppressing live block delivery and partial streaming when captioned final TTS will be used, and correctly handling tagged auto-TTS mode with a split eligibility boolean.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. Block text / partial streaming delivers text to user immediately
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered as separate message
  Result: user sees text first, then voice note = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true (channel supports captioned voice + final TTS mode)
  2. Partial streaming suppressed, block text delivery suppressed
  3. TTS synthesis runs, text accumulated silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message (or text fallback if TTS fails)
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

### Tagged auto-TTS mode

When `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`, TTS only fires for replies containing `[[tts:...]]` directives. Three additional issues were discovered and fixed:

1. **Split boolean**: `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` was doing two jobs ŌĆö gating live text suppression AND caption eligibility. Split into `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` (live text suppression, excludes tagged) and `canCaptionFinalTts` (caption eligibility, includes tagged).

2. **Block TTS gate**: The accumulated block TTS fallback was gated on `replies.length === 0`, but Anthropic streaming returns both block callbacks and a final reply payload. Changed to gate on `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` and `queuedFinal` instead.

3. **Partial streaming leak**: `onPartialReply` was active for tagged mode, sending raw `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directive text to Telegram during streaming, creating a duplicate text message before the voice+caption arrived. Fixed by gating `onPartialReply` with `canCaptionFinalTts` (covers both always and tagged modes) instead of `willUseCaptionedFinalTts`.

### Media block preservation

When captioned final TTS is active, block suppression is narrowed to **text-only** payloads. Blocks containing media (`mediaUrl`, `mediaUrls`), `presentation`, `interactive`, or `channelData` are delivered immediately with their text stripped (to avoid duplicating the caption). This applies to both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` + `canCaptionFinalTts`: suppress partial streaming and block text for both always and tagged modes; narrow block suppression to text-only payloads (media blocks delivered with text stripped); `sendFinalPayload` returns `deliveredMedia`; block TTS gate changed from `replies.length === 0` to `!deliveredFinalTtsMedia && !queuedFinal` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | Regression tests: tagged directive-only voice+caption, tagged+always both-block-and-final delivery, partial suppression for tagged TTS on caption-capable channels, plain tagged block delivery, media block delivery with text stripped, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` gate: suppress ACP block delivery and defer text settlement; `canCaptionFinalTts` for tagged mode; `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` tracking |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` state tracking + `hasDeliveredFinalTtsMedia()` accessor; narrow `suppressBlockUserDelivery` to text-only blocks (media blocks delivered with text stripped) |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts` | Regression tests: media block delivery with text stripped under `suppressBlockUserDelivery`, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | Regression tests: deferred settlement, block suppression, tagged streaming, tagged directive TTS, ACP live text deferral |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | `deferLiveTextUntilFinalFlush` option to suppress live text flushing for tagged TTS |
| `src/tts/directives.ts` | `resolveDirectiveOnlyTtsCaptionText` parser helper to extract clean caption from directive-only responses |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `docs/tools/tts.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability: opt-in criteria via `capabilities.tts.voice`, tagged mode behavior, text-only fallback, plugin API snippet |
| `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability in channel plugin SDK guide: opt-in declaration, media block preservation, fallback behavior, tagged mode |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |

## Tests

```
$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 Ō£ō 202 passed (202)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts
 Ō£ō 29 passed (29)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 Ō£ō 50 passed (50)

$ npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 Ō£ō 11 passed (11)

$ npx vitest run src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 Ō£ō 1 passed (1)

Total: 293 tests passed across 5 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode text-before-voice churn on both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths, plus tagged mode partial preview leaking raw `[[tts:text]]` directives, plus media block suppression during captioned final TTS.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `05b4483a8f`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix ŌĆö separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-before-fix.png)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message AND a voice note ŌĆö instead of a single voice note with caption.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Built and installed from current head: `npm run build && npm pack && npm install -g openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz`
  2. Registered and restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway install && openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "always"` and `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"` ŌĆö sent plain messages and directive-bearing messages to `@thomas_fu_bot` on Telegram

- **Evidence after fix**: Always mode (`messages.tts.auto: "always"`) at commit `05b4483a8f`:

  ![Always mode ŌĆö voice with caption, single message](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-always-mode.png)

  Always-mode replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No text churn, no separate text bubble.

- **Evidence ŌĆö Media block preservation with captioned final TTS at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Media block preserved ŌĆö image delivered + voice note with caption](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-media-block.png)

  When the bot generates an image during a TTS-enabled conversation (`auto: "always"`), the image is delivered immediately while text is deferred to the voice note caption. No media blocks are suppressed, and caption text does not duplicate as a separate text message.

- **Observed result after fix**: Always TTS auto mode delivers voice notes as single messages with caption text on Telegram. Media blocks (images, files) are preserved and delivered immediately with text stripped. No text flash, no duplicate messages, no media loss.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS (tested in DM only). Other channels with `captionedFinalText` capability (only Telegram tested). Tagged mode live proof pending ŌĆö covered by unit tests (partial suppression + directive-only caption regression tests added).

Closes #83511

target.title: fix(feishu): carry forward DM fallback and topic labels
target.body:
## Summary

Carry forward the remaining Feishu display-name behavior from #38958 after #51032 already landed the group-name session label fix.

This replacement should stay narrow:
- keep #51032's group-name implementation as the baseline
- add/repair DM display-name fallback through chatMembers with contact fallback
- preserve resolveSenderNames opt-out semantics
- scope sender/direct-member caches by account and relevant chat/sender identifiers
- surface topic/root labels only for topic-scoped Feishu sessions
- propagate permission guidance when DM fallback lookup hits a Feishu permission error

Credit: this work is based on the implementation direction and review iterations from @futuremind2026 in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/38958. The already-merged group-name baseline came from @jnuyao in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/51032.

## Validation

- pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot-sender-name.test.ts
- pnpm exec vitest run src/config/sessions.test.ts
- pnpm check:changed

ProjectClownfish replacement details:
- Cluster: ghcrawl-156980-autonomous-smoke
- Source PRs: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/38958
- Credit: Credit @futuremind2026 and source PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/38958 for the broader Feishu DM fallback, cache-scoping, permission guidance, and topic-label implementation direction.; Preserve @jnuyao and merged PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/51032 as the landed Feishu group-name implementation; do not overwrite or duplicate that work.; Mention in the PR body that this replacement carries forward the remaining behavior from #38958 because ProjectClownfish cannot safely update the original contributor branch.
- Validation: pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot-sender-name.test.ts; pnpm exec vitest run src/config/sessions.test.ts; pnpm check:changed

The PR injects `OPENCLAW_AGENT_ID` and `OPENCLAW_SESSION_KEY` into exec child-process environments across gateway/sandbox/PTY, node-host, and macOS companion paths, with docs and regression tests.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +121, Docs +3, Other +11. Total +146 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main carries agent/session context through exec paths but only injects `OPENCLAW_SHELL` into child env. I did not run a live exec session in this read-only review.

target.title: feat(exec): inject OPENCLAW_SESSION_KEY and OPENCLAW_AGENT_ID env vars for child processes
target.body:
## Summary
Inject `OPENCLAW_SESSION_KEY` and `OPENCLAW_AGENT_ID` environment variables into child processes spawned by the exec tool, allowing scripts and skills to reliably identify the current session and agent without relying on model parameter passing.

Supersedes #68859 with full coverage including node-host execution, regression tests, and docs updates.

Closes #66705

## Problem
Currently, skills and scripts executed via exec have no reliable way to determine which session or agent they belong to. While the model can pass these identifiers as parameters, this approach is prone to hallucination errors where the model may provide incorrect values.

This blocks workflows where a child process needs to register a callback or webhook that routes back to the originating session.

## Solution
Automatically inject `OPENCLAW_SESSION_KEY` and `OPENCLAW_AGENT_ID` into the environment of child processes spawned by exec, across all execution paths.

### Changes

**Core injection:**
- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.ts` ŌĆö inject both vars in `runExecProcess` (`shellRuntimeEnv`)
- `src/node-host/invoke-system-run.ts` ŌĆö inject both vars in `shellRuntimeEnv` after sanitization for `host=node` execution
- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.ts` ŌĆö pass `agentId` through gateway allowlist path

**Type updates:**
- Added `agentId?: string` to `runExecProcess` opts type

**Documentation:**
- `docs/help/environment.md` ŌĆö document both new env vars
- `docs/tools/exec.md` ŌĆö note session/agent injection for all exec hosts

**Tests:**
- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts` ŌĆö 6 new tests covering gateway injection, user env override prevention, and empty-string clearing semantics
- `src/node-host/invoke-system-run.test.ts` ŌĆö updated sanitizer allowlist test to verify trusted var injection
- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts` ŌĆö removed stale test (gateway no longer injects vars into `requestedEnv`)

## Testing
- [x] Local gateway execution (host=gateway)
- [x] Sandbox execution (host=sandbox) ŌĆö covered by shared shellRuntimeEnv path
- [x] PTY mode (pty=true) ŌĆö covered by shared shellRuntimeEnv path
- [x] Node-host execution (host=node) ŌĆö unit tests in invoke-system-run.test.ts
- [x] Override semantics ŌĆö user env cannot override trusted values
- [x] Clearing semantics ŌĆö empty string when no agent/session defaults provided

## Usage Example
```python
import os
session_key = os.environ.get("OPENCLAW_SESSION_KEY")
agent_id = os.environ.get("OPENCLAW_AGENT_ID")
```

## Backward Compatibility
Fully backward compatible:
- Variables always set (empty string when not available), avoiding stale-value issues
- Trusted values override any user-provided env overrides with the same names
- No existing behavior changed

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Inject OPENCLAW_SESSION_KEY and OPENCLAW_AGENT_ID environment variables into exec child processes so skills and scripts can reliably identify their session and agent without relying on model parameter passing.

**Real environment tested**: OpenClaw 2026.4.2, macOS arm64, gateway mode

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
1. Built and ran OpenClaw gateway with the patched code
2. Created a minimal test skill at workspace/skills/test-env-injection/ that prints both environment variables
3. Executed the skill script via the exec tool within a main agent session
4. Captured the console output

**Evidence after fix**:
```bash
$ python3 ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/test-env-injection/test.py
agent:main:2
main
```

**Observed result after fix**: Both OPENCLAW_SESSION_KEY and OPENCLAW_AGENT_ID are correctly injected into the exec child process environment. The output matches the expected session key (agent:main:2) and agent id (main).

**What was not tested**: Node-host (host=node) execution path was not tested in a real multi-node setup; only unit tests cover this path.

target.title: fix: prevent silent message loss from EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError
target.body:
## Summary

When the OpenAI SDK retried an `ECONNRESET` internally (default `maxRetries=2`), the session write lock was released during the retry window. Incoming steering messages could then modify the transcript without updating the fence, causing a fingerprint mismatch on `reacquireAfterPrompt()`. The resulting `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` was caught by `model-fallback.ts` which aborted the run, and the outer reply pipeline produced no user-visible output ŌĆö the message was silently lost.

## Root Cause Chain

```
API ECONNRESET
  ŌåÆ OpenAI SDK default maxRetries=2, internal retry
  ŌåÆ session write lock released during retry window
  ŌåÆ incoming message arrives via queueHandle.queueMessage
  ŌåÆ activeSession.steer writes directly (bypassing withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites)
  ŌåÆ transcript changes without updating fence
  ŌåÆ original attempt reacquireAfterPrompt() fingerprint mismatch
  ŌåÆ EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError thrown
  ŌåÆ model-fallback.ts aborts the run
  ŌåÆ outer layer hits generic failure branch
  ŌåÆ assistantTexts/payloads never enter buildReplyPayloads()
  ŌåÆ message silently lost (user sees generic error or nothing)
```

## Fix

Five coordinated changes that address the bug at multiple layers:

### 1. Wrap steering in `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites` (`attempt.ts`)
Steering writes that arrive during prompt lock release now go through `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, keeping fence tracking consistent and preventing the fingerprint mismatch that triggers the takeover error.

### 2. Dedicated error handler for takeover (`agent-runner-execution.ts`, `failover-error.ts`)
Added `isEmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError()` export and a dedicated catch block in `runAgentTurnWithFallback`. Instead of falling into the generic failure branch (silent loss), users now receive a clear message asking them to resend. When a takeover wraps a preserved prompt error (billing, rate-limit, context-overflow), the original error is unwrapped and classified normally ŌĆö only pure takeover errors return resend guidance.

### 3. Scope SDK retry disable to embedded prompt lock path (`attempt.ts`, `attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Extracted `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` helper that wraps the session's `streamFn` with `{ maxRetries: 0, ...options }` ŌĆö the `0` is a **default** that goes before the caller options spread, so `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` (surfaced via `options.maxRetries` per the chain in `sdk.ts:360`) takes precedence when explicitly configured. Non-embedded callers retain the SDK default retry behavior (maxRetries=2).

### 4. Forward maxRetries through native OpenAI transport (`openai-transport-stream.ts`)
`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` previously accepted only `model` and `signal`, dropping the `maxRetries` injected by `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault`. Added `maxRetries` parameter and forwarded it through all three native transport call sites (Responses, Azure Responses, Completions). When `maxRetries` is the only option present, the function now returns an object instead of `undefined` so the SDK receives the override.

### 5. Phase tracking in `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` (`attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Added a `phase` field (`prompt_reacquire`, `cleanup`, `write`, `fence_check`) for better diagnostics when this error does occur. Backward compatible ŌĆö the phase parameter is optional with existing call sites unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

> **Refreshed at current head `bf4c277c9b` (supersedes the v6 proof below, which targeted older head `2fc3ecee2d`).**

- **Behavior addressed**: A session takeover during the embedded prompt-lock release window must not silently drop the user's reply, and the injected embedded retry default must refresh with the latest configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` across repeated installs (not freeze the first-captured value).
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `bf4c277c9b`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, freshly installed deps (`pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran the affected suites at this head with `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs <suite>` ŌĆö the organic session-takeover regression (real on-disk session + real local HTTP model server + real OpenAI-completions transport + real prompt-lock fence), the retry-default live-ref unit suite (V1ŌĆōV5), and the reply-runtime regression ŌĆö plus `tsgo:test:src` for typecheck.
- **Evidence after fix**: Copied live terminal output captured at head `bf4c277c9b`:
```
 Ō£ō surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError  724ms
 Ō£ō src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts (72 tests) 141ms
      Tests  72 passed (72)
      Tests  205 passed (205)
 tsgo:test:src exits 0 with no errors
```
- **Observed result after fix**: The organic session takeover now surfaces explicit resend guidance ("ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Your message was interrupted because new input arrived while the model was retrying a connection error. Please resend your message.") instead of silently dropping the reply; the retry-default wrapper reads the shared live ref at call time so a repeat install refreshes the next call (including downward refresh when the configured value drops); and typecheck is clean at this head.
- **What was not tested**: The live Feishu/DeepSeek ECONNRESET timing race that originally surfaced #87180 is not reproduced here; the takeover is reproduced deterministically via a real on-disk fence-drift, which exercises the same `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` path. The CI matrix on this head was still running at proof time.


### Current-head proof (`bf4c277c9b`)

- **Behavior addressed**: A session takeover during the embedded prompt-lock release window must not silently drop the user's reply, and the injected embedded retry default must refresh with the latest configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` across repeated installs (not freeze the first-captured value).
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `bf4c277c9b`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, freshly installed deps (`pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`).
- **Why refreshed**: The previous proof targeted an older head; the retry-default wrapper was reworked to read a shared live ref at call time (commit on this head), so proof is regenerated on the resulting head.

### 1. Organic session-takeover regression (real on-disk session + real local HTTP model server + real OpenAI-completions transport + real prompt-lock fence)
```
     Ō£ō surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError  724ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
```
The takeover error is thrown organically by the real prompt-lock fence (a concurrent steering write rewrites the same session file mid-call), and `runAgentTurnWithFallback` surfaces resend guidance instead of dropping the reply.

### 2. Retry-default live-ref unit proof (the wrapper fix, V1ŌĆōV5)
```
 Ō£ō  agents  src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts (72 tests) 141ms
      Tests  72 passed (72)
```
Covers: refresh through the buried wrapper after lock-release wraps it; call-time live read so a repeat install changes the next call; downward refresh when the configured value drops (the original bug's essence); shared ref identity across both install orders; and no wrapper stacking on repeated installs.

### 3. Reply-runtime regression (no behavior regression on the takeover catch path)
```
      Tests  205 passed (205)
```

### Typecheck
`tsgo:test:src` exits 0 with no errors at this head.

### What was not tested
The live Feishu/DeepSeek ECONNRESET timing race that originally surfaced #87180 is not reproduced here; the takeover is reproduced deterministically via a real on-disk fence-drift, which exercises the same `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` path. The CI matrix on this head was still running at proof time.


<details>
<summary>Earlier proof (v6, head 2fc3ecee2d) ŌĆö historical</summary>


- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Silent message loss on session takeover during the released prompt-lock window. When the OpenAI SDK retried an `ECONNRESET` internally, an incoming steering write changed the transcript without updating the fence, raising `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`, which `model-fallback.ts` aborted into a generic failure branch so the user saw no actionable output. After this fix the pure-takeover path returns explicit resend guidance, the SDK retry default is scoped to the embedded prompt-lock window (with configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` taking precedence), and `maxRetries` is forwarded through the native OpenAI transport.
- **Real environment tested**: Clean Docker container `node:24-bookworm` (Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64) running the patched checkout at the current head commit `2fc3ecee2d`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Built the patched checkout in the container and ran the affected suites plus a real-setup organic takeover test (real on-disk session file + real local `node:http` model server on an `openai-completions` baseUrl + real transport + real fence) and code-path verification, as captured in the v6 proof log below.
- **Evidence after fix**: Copied live terminal output from the patched container at head `2fc3ecee2d` (full v6 log reproduced in the section below):

```
$ docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/app" -w /app node:24-bookworm bash /proof.sh
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v6, current head) ===
Environment: Docker container, v24.16.0, aarch64, "Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
Commit: 2fc3ecee2d
--- A: Real-setup organic session-takeover proof (resend guidance) ---
 Ō£ō surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError 1456ms
AFTER-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Your message was interrupted because new input arrived while the model was retrying a connection error. Please resend your message.
BEFORE-fix user-facing payload text (exact, parent 86150a3e51):
ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Something went wrong while processing your request. Please try again, or use /new to start a fresh session.
=== PROOF COMPLETE @ 2fc3ecee2d ===
```
- **Observed result after fix**: The organic session takeover now surfaces a specific, actionable resend message (the after-fix payload line above) instead of the generic non-actionable failure seen at the parent; the configured provider retry survives through `sdk.ts` to the native SDK request options; the native transport forwards `maxRetries`; and the retry-default installer is idempotent with caller > configured > 0 precedence.
- **What was not tested**: No live multi-user deployment against a real upstream provider with an actual network-level `ECONNRESET` was run. The takeover proof uses a real on-disk session file, a real local HTTP model server, the real transport, and the real fence (organic error), with only the upstream provider socket and the "second user" steering write synthesized; the supplementary transport proof relies on unit/loopback tests in a real Docker container.

### Current-head proof (commit `2fc3ecee2d`, Docker `node:24-bookworm`)

Refreshed at the latest head, which adds the configured-provider-retry precedence. Run in a clean Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`, Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64):

```
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v6, current head) ===
Environment: Docker container, v24.16.0, aarch64, "Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
Commit: 2fc3ecee2d

--- A: Real-setup organic session-takeover proof (resend guidance) ---
 Ō£ō auto-reply  agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts (1 test)
   Ō£ō surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError 1456ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)

--- B: Embedded prompt retry default ŌĆö idempotence + configured-override precedence ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  51 passed (51)

--- C: Configured provider retry survives through sdk.ts to native SDK options ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)

--- D: Transport forwards maxRetries (native OpenAI) + failover classification ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)    Tests  83 passed (83)
 Test Files  1 passed (1)    Tests  241 passed (241)

--- E: Code-path verification at current head ---
installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault accepts configured default:
1088:  options?: { maxRetries?: number },
1098:  const defaultMaxRetries = options?.maxRetries ?? 0;
Install site passes configured provider retry:
3994:              maxRetries: settingsManager.getProviderRetrySettings().maxRetries,

=== PROOF COMPLETE @ 2fc3ecee2d ===
```

This confirms at the current head: the organic session-takeover path still yields visible resend guidance (A); the retry-default installer is idempotent and applies the configured-override precedence caller > configured > 0 (B); a configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` survives through `sdk.ts` to the native SDK request options (C, the new regression); and the native transport forwards `maxRetries` (D).

### Real session-takeover path (organic error, real transport + real fence)

This proof drives the actual user-visible takeover path end to end and captures the real user-facing reply, rather than relying only on focused unit tests. Committed as `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts`.

**What is real (not hand-built):**
- A real on-disk session transcript file in a temp dir.
- A real local `node:http` server as the model `baseUrl` (`api: openai-completions`) that announces in-flight, then blocks before finishing the SSE body.
- The real `openai-completions` transport doing real HTTP I/O.
- The real embedded prompt-lock controller + fence (`createEmbeddedAttemptSessionLockController`), wired through the real `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` / `installPromptSubmissionLockRelease` helpers ŌĆö the exact helpers `attempt.ts` uses in production.
- The `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` is thrown **organically** by `assertSessionFileFence("prompt_reacquire")` when a concurrent steering write changes the session-file fingerprint during the released-lock window ŌĆö it is **not** constructed by hand.
- The real `isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError` classification and the real `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch branch producing the user-facing payload.

**Mechanism (deterministic Promise handshakes, no sleeps):**
1. A real temp session file is created with one session line.
2. On the first model request the mock server resolves a `firstRequestReceived` deferred (the model call is in-flight), then blocks on a `releaseServer` deferred before writing the body.
3. `installPromptSubmissionLockRelease` calls `releaseForPrompt()` at the start of the model call (opening the takeover window) and drains the real transport stream, so the released-lock window spans the real HTTP block.
4. While blocked, the test performs a concurrent "second user" steering write to the **same** real session file, changing its fingerprint.
5. `releaseServer` resolves; the server finishes the SSE body; the wrapped `finally{}` calls `reacquireAfterPrompt()` ŌåÆ `assertSessionFileFence("prompt_reacquire")`.
6. The fingerprint mismatch trips the fence ŌåÆ `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` thrown organically ŌåÆ real classification ŌåÆ real `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch.

**Before / after (run at this PR's parent `86150a3e51` in a separate worktree; the session-lock + fence code is byte-identical between parent and HEAD, so the organic error reproduces identically ŌĆö the only difference is the runner's new catch branch):**

```
AFTER fix (HEAD) ŌĆö vitest verbose
 RUN  v4.1.7
 Ō£ō |auto-reply| agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts > surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError 487ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
AFTER-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Your message was interrupted because new input arrived while the model was retrying a connection error. Please resend your message.

BEFORE fix (parent 86150a3e51, separate worktree) ŌĆö vitest verbose
 RUN  v4.1.7
 Ō£ō |auto-reply| agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts > BEFORE FIX: organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError yields no resend guidance 483ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
BEFORE-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Something went wrong while processing your request. Please try again, or use /new to start a fresh session.
```

**What this proves:** an organic session takeover during a real released-lock model call produces, after this fix, a specific actionable resend message instead of the generic non-actionable failure the user saw before. Honest note: at this PR's immediate parent the before-fix payload is the **generic** failure text shown above (not literal empty output); the originally reported "silent message loss" is the production symptom of that non-actionable failure path. The change at this seam converts the generic failure into specific resend guidance so the user knows their steering message must be resent.

### Supplementary: transport + retry-suppression proof (Docker `node:24-bookworm`)

- **Behavior addressed**: Silent message loss when SDK retries race with session takeover ŌåÆ visible resend guidance for pure takeover, correct error classification for wrapped prompt errors, provider retry settings respected, maxRetries forwarded through native OpenAI transport
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`, Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64) running patched checkout at commit `80bbe803e2`
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` only accepts `model` and `signal` ŌĆö it drops `maxRetries`. Embedded prompt lock window injects `maxRetries: 0` but the native OpenAI transport never forwards it to the SDK, so SDK retries (default `maxRetries=2`) still occur during the released lock window, widening the race. Additionally, `queueMessage` steering writes bypass `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, causing fingerprint mismatch ŌåÆ `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` ŌåÆ generic failure branch ŌåÆ silent message loss.
- **Exact steps and commands run after this patch**:

```
$ docker run --rm -e CI=true -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app node:24-bookworm bash /proof.sh
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v5) ===
Environment: Docker container, v24.16.0, aarch64, "Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
Commit: 80bbe803e2

--- Installing dependencies (pnpm) ---
install exit: 0
Done in 47s using pnpm v11.2.2

--- Test 1: Native OpenAI transport forwards maxRetries ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  241 passed (241)

--- Test 2: Embedded prompt lock defaults + override ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  48 passed (48)

--- Test 3: Takeover error handling + unwrapping (agent-runner-execution) ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  184 passed (184)

--- Test 4: failover-error classification ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  83 passed (83)

--- Test 5: SDK transport paths (openai-completions + openai-responses-shared) ---
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
 Tests  32 passed (32)

--- Test 6: Code path verification ---
buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions accepts+forwards maxRetries:
1843:  maxRetries?: number,
1844:): { signal?: AbortSignal; timeout?: number; maxRetries?: number } | undefined {
1846:  if (timeout === undefined && !signal && maxRetries === undefined) {
1852:    ...(maxRetries !== undefined ? { maxRetries } : {}),
1940:          options?.maxRetries,
2380:          options?.maxRetries,
2614:          buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions(model, options?.signal, options?.maxRetries),

Embedded retry default (maxRetries:0 BEFORE options spread):
1083:    return innerStreamFn(model, context, { maxRetries: 0, ...requestOptions });
```

- **Evidence after fix**: All 588 tests pass across the 6 affected test suites in a clean Docker environment.
  - **Transport retry suppression proven end-to-end**: A loopback regression test in `openai-transport-stream.test.ts` starts a mock server returning retryable HTTP 500 errors, passes `maxRetries: 0` through the full native completions transport path, and asserts exactly 1 request is received ŌĆö confirming the SDK does not retry when `maxRetries: 0` is forwarded through the native transport.
  - Default SDK retry behavior preserved for non-embedded callers (no `maxRetries` in default request options).
  - Embedded prompt lock window correctly injects `maxRetries: 0` as a default that configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` can override.
  - Native OpenAI transport (`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions`) correctly forwards `maxRetries` to all 3 SDK call sites.
  - Pure takeover errors produce visible resend guidance. Wrapped prompt errors are unwrapped and classified normally.
- **What was not tested**: No live multi-user deployment against a real upstream provider with an actual network-level ECONNRESET was run. The takeover path proof uses a real on-disk session file, a real local HTTP model server, the real transport, and the real fence (the error is organic), with only the upstream provider socket and the "second user" steering write synthesized; the supplementary transport proof relies on unit/loopback tests in a real Docker container.


</details>

## Tests

All affected test suites pass (Docker `node:24-bookworm`, aarch64):

| Test File | Tests |
|-----------|-------|
| `agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts` (real-setup takeover proof) | 1 |
| `openai-transport-stream.test.ts` | 241 |
| `attempt.session-lock.test.ts` | 48 |
| `agent-runner-execution.test.ts` | 184 |
| `failover-error.test.ts` | 83 |
| `openai-completions.test.ts` + `openai-responses-shared.test.ts` | 32 |

New test coverage includes:

target.title: fix(mattermost): channel prompts describe rooms as group chats
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes Mattermost channel prompt guidance so channel conversations are described consistently as channels, not partially as group chats.

- Fix classification: Root-cause prompt builder invariant fix.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High.
- Root cause: The shared group/channel prompt helper receives trusted `ChatType: "channel"` for Mattermost channel rooms, but its delivery, participant, task, and tool-only guidance used hard-coded group-chat labels. That source prompt builder produced contradictory channel-vs-group instructions.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The fix changes the source prompt builder invariant so trusted `ChatType` selects one channel/group label set before any guidance is appended. Every trusted guidance fragment now consumes that source label set, so the prompt cannot mix `Mattermost channel` with `this group chat` for channel sessions.
- Patch quality notes: Small focused patch quality guardrail: one source file plus one regression test file, no catch-all fallback, no string-scrubbing downstream, no schema/config/protocol changes, and ClawSweeper commit review found nothing actionable.

## Linked context

Related: #95645

The issue reports that Mattermost public/private channels receive prompt intro wording that calls the conversation a group chat even though the authoritative metadata identifies the room as a channel.

Linked-open-PR scan note: external linked PRs were treated as advisory only; this change stays narrow and does not touch public API, config, schema, protocol, delivery routing, or Mattermost event ingestion.

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users relying on Mattermost channel auto-reply behavior would get system prompt guidance that mixed channel wording with group-chat delivery instructions when the incoming room was a channel.

- Behavior or issue addressed: Mattermost channel prompts now consistently describe the visible destination as a channel, including normal text delivery, message-tool delivery, participant behavior, delegated task guidance, and no-visible-response guidance.
- Why it matters / User impact: The model receives a single trusted description of the conversation type, avoiding contradictory prompt context that could make a channel response sound or behave like a group-chat response.

## Why This Change Was Made

Group and channel conversations share the same helper. The safest boundary is to keep that routing unchanged and make the helper derive all destination labels from trusted `ChatType` once.

- What did NOT change: This does not change message delivery routing, Mattermost event handling, session state, permissions, provider adapters, public API, config, schema, protocol, defaults, direct conversation behavior, or the wording for existing group conversations.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: `ChatType` remains the source of truth for channel-vs-group prompt wording. The PR does not add a second classifier or infer channel semantics from provider-specific strings.

## User Impact

Mattermost channel replies now get consistent channel wording throughout the system prompt. Existing group conversations keep the previous group-chat wording.

## Real behavior proof

Before the fix, `buildGroupChatContext({ sessionCtx: { ChatType: "channel", Provider: "mattermost" } })` could produce mixed guidance such as `You are in a Mattermost channel` together with group-specific phrases like `this group chat`, `same group`, and `group-chat task`.

After the fix, the regression test asserts the Mattermost channel prompt contains channel-specific guidance for normal delivery and tool-only delivery, and forbids the residual group-specific fragments for `ChatType: "channel"`.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran focused auto-reply prompt/inbound metadata tests, Mattermost monitor tests, and `git diff --check` against the current committed patch.
- Evidence after fix: `accepted-auto-reply-tests-v2` passed 2 files / 63 tests, `mattermost-tests-v2` passed 2 files / 14 tests, `diff-check-v2` passed, and ClawSweeper commit review returned `nothing_found`.
- Observed result after fix: The new regression coverage confirms Mattermost `ChatType: "channel"` prompt output includes `Mattermost channel`, `this channel`, `same channel`, `channel participant`, and `channel task`, while excluding `Mattermost group chat`, `this group chat`, `same group`, `group-chat task`, and `group response` on the channel path.
- What was not tested: No live Mattermost server was started; this issue is source-reproducible in the prompt builder and covered with focused unit/regression tests plus existing Mattermost monitor tests.

Evidence after fix: Validation `accepted-auto-reply-tests-v2` (pass, exit_code=0, 12120ms):

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 [local path redacted]


 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  63 passed (63)
   Start at  20:03:50
   Duration  1.29s (transform 1.01s, setup 998ms, import 459ms, tests 311ms, environment 1ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 11.81s
```

Evidence after fix: Validation `mattermost-tests-v2` (pass, exit_code=0, 33797ms):

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-mattermost.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 [local path redacted]


 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  14 passed (14)
   Start at  20:04:05
   Duration  21.89s (transform 12.90s, setup 1.29s, import 20.15s, tests 837ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 33.39s
```

Evidence after fix: Validation `diff-check-v2` (pass, exit_code=0, 136ms):

```text
Validation result: pass, exit_code=0, 136ms
```

ClawSweeper commit review: `result: nothing_found`, `check_conclusion: success`, `highest_severity: none`.

## Evidence

The real behavior proof and validation output above are the maintainer-visible evidence for this source-repro prompt bug.

## Tests and validation

- Target test file: `src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts`, with related focused coverage in `src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts` and Mattermost monitor tests.
- Scenario locked in: A Mattermost channel event with trusted `ChatType: "channel"` builds the shared group/channel context and must receive channel-specific wording throughout the prompt.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The test checks both positive channel wording and negative group-wording exclusions in normal and tool-only paths, directly guarding the source helper without adding brittle downstream prompt scrubbing.

## Risk checklist

- Risk labels considered: automation, message-delivery, security-boundary, session-state.
- Risk explanation: The changed strings describe whether replies are visible, private, or sent through the message tool, so they sit near delivery and automation guidance even though they are prompt text only.
- Why acceptable: The patch changes only prompt labels derived from the already-trusted `ChatType`; it does not alter routing, sending, session persistence, permissions, authentication, provider adapters, config, schema, protocol, or defaults.
- Compatibility/default behavior: Existing group conversations keep the previous group-chat wording, and non-channel chat types continue through the existing group path unchanged.
- Out of scope: No Mattermost event ingestion changes, no metadata shape changes, no public API/config/schema changes, no delivery semantics changes, and no prompt behavior changes for direct conversations.

## Official template answers

### What problem does this PR solve?

Mattermost channel prompts no longer mix channel wording with group-chat delivery guidance.

### Why does this matter now?

The issue is user-visible in the prompt context for Mattermost channel rooms and can give the model contradictory instructions about where replies are posted.

### What is the intended outcome?

Root-cause fix: the source prompt builder derives all group/channel wording from trusted `ChatType` before appending guidance.

### What is intentionally out of scope?

Changing Mattermost ingestion, delivery routing, session persistence, provider adapters, public API, config, schema, protocol, defaults, or direct-chat behavior is intentionally out of scope.

### Which commands did you run?

`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor-gating.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.inbound-system-event.test.ts`; `git diff --check`.

### What regression coverage was added or updated?

Updated `src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts` to lock Mattermost `ChatType: "channel"` wording for normal and tool-only prompt paths, including negative checks against the former group-specific fragments.

### What is the highest-risk area?

The highest-risk area is prompt text adjacent to automation and message-delivery guidance.

### How is that risk mitigated?

The patch changes only labels selected from trusted `ChatType`, preserves existing routing and delivery semantics, and includes focused positive/negative regression checks plus existing Mattermost monitor tests.

### What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer review.

### Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local ClawSweeper commit review previously flagged residual group-specific channel wording; the updated patch addresses that by deriving channel/group labels once and applying them across all trusted guidance fragments.

## Current review state

Ready for maintainer review.

Local validation is passing, maintainer-visible proof is included above, and ClawSweeper commit review found no actionable issues.

target.title: fix(feishu): normalize unicode emojis to Feishu emoji type strings for reactions API (#66406)
target.body:
## Summary
Feishu reactions API returns error `231001` ("reaction type is invalid") because the agent sends raw unicode emojis (e.g. thumbs-up `­¤æŹ`) but the API requires uppercase Feishu type strings like `THUMBSUP`.

## Root Cause
In `extensions/feishu/src/channel.ts`, the `emoji` parameter from the tool call is passed directly to `addReactionFeishu()` without conversion. The Feishu API only accepts its own emoji type vocabulary (e.g. `THUMBSUP`, `HEART`, `FIRE`) per https://open.feishu.cn/document/server-docs/im-v1/message-reaction/emojis-introduce, not unicode characters.

ProjectClownfish landed a narrow repair on this branch on 2026-04-28 (force-push by `vincentkoc`) so the original contributor path can stay canonical. The fix shape and tests have been stable since then.

## Changes
- `extensions/feishu/src/reactions.ts`: Add `normalizeFeishuEmoji()` function with a unicode-to-Feishu-type mapping table covering all common emoji reactions. Known Feishu type strings pass through unchanged; case-insensitive matching is supported.
- `extensions/feishu/src/channel.ts`: Apply `normalizeFeishuEmoji()` to the emoji parameter before calling `addReactionFeishu()` and `listReactionsFeishu()`.
- `extensions/feishu/src/reactions.test.ts`: Add test coverage for pass-through, case normalization, unicode conversion, whitespace trimming, and unknown emoji fallback.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Tool calls that pass unicode emoji to `addReactionFeishu` send `{ reaction: { emoji_type: "­¤æŹ" } }` to the Feishu reactions HTTP API, which rejects it with `code: 231001` and `msg: "reaction type is invalid"`. After the fix the same tool call sends `{ reaction: { emoji_type: "THUMBSUP" } }`, which is the documented Feishu emoji type vocabulary. Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/66406
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-66406` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. Because the worktree's `extensions/feishu/src/reactions.ts` imports run-time Feishu SDK modules transitively, the smoke script extracts the **production `FeishuEmoji` constant table and `normalizeFeishuEmoji` function body verbatim** from `extensions/feishu/src/reactions.ts` (SHA-256-pinned for integrity) and executes that exact slice via `node --experimental-strip-types`. No mocks, no test runner ŌĆö the live function body is what runs. PR head: `a3bebf502f1ce0ebfe3b754ec1ab67b3302098fc`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `git checkout fix/feishu-reaction-emoji-type && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr66478.mts`. The smoke script reads `extensions/feishu/src/reactions.ts`, slices from `export const FeishuEmoji` to end-of-file, prints the prod-slice SHA-256, then exercises `normalizeFeishuEmoji` against the actual reporter unicode emojis.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3bebf502f` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, 2245-byte production slice verified by SHA-256 `8e5dd9eb5cc3406b058e81617e0085a2347b780ad8d49b3220aab683c243af54`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr66478.mts
$ wc -c C:\...\feishu-combined-1778492490068.mts
  bytes: 3667
(prod slice: 2245 bytes copied verbatim from extensions/feishu/src/reactions.ts starting at offset 3989)
(SHA-256 of prod slice for integrity:)
  8e5dd9eb5cc3406b058e81617e0085a2347b780ad8d49b3220aab683c243af54

=== Sample known Feishu emoji constants ===
FeishuEmoji.THUMBSUP = THUMBSUP
FeishuEmoji.HEART    = HEART
FeishuEmoji.CRY      = CRY

=== Unicode emoji -> Feishu type name ===
  "­¤æŹ"             (thumbs up)  ->  THUMBSUP
  "ŌØż’ĖÅ"             (red heart)  ->  HEART
  "­¤śé"             (face with tears of joy)  ->  LAUGHING
  "­¤Äē"             (party popper)  ->  PARTY
  "­¤ÖÅ"             (folded hands)  ->  PRAY
  "­¤öź"             (fire)  ->  FIRE

=== Bypass cases (must NOT be touched) ===
  "THUMBSUP"      -> "THUMBSUP"
  "thumbsup"      -> "THUMBSUP"
  "ThumbsUp"      -> "THUMBSUP"
  "  HEART  "     -> "HEART"
  "UNKNOWN_TYPE"  -> "UNKNOWN_TYPE"

=== Request body produced for issue #66406 reproducer ===
user sends: "­¤æŹ"
request body: {"reaction":{"emoji_type":"THUMBSUP"}}
~~~

- **Observed result after fix**: Each tested unicode emoji is mapped to a documented Feishu type name (`­¤æŹ`ŌåÆ`THUMBSUP`, `ŌØż’ĖÅ`ŌåÆ`HEART`, `­¤śé`ŌåÆ`LAUGHING`, `­¤Äē`ŌåÆ`PARTY`, `­¤ÖÅ`ŌåÆ`PRAY`, `­¤öź`ŌåÆ`FIRE`) ŌĆö exactly the vocabulary listed at https://open.feishu.cn/document/server-docs/im-v1/message-reaction/emojis-introduce. Existing uppercase Feishu types (`THUMBSUP`), lowercase aliases (`thumbsup`), mixed case (`ThumbsUp`), and whitespace-padded inputs (`"  HEART  "`) all pass through to their canonical type name. Unknown inputs (`UNKNOWN_TYPE`) fall through unchanged, preserving forward-compat with new Feishu types. The final reproducer line confirms the runtime request body now contains `"emoji_type":"THUMBSUP"` ŌĆö which is exactly what the Feishu API requires to avoid `code: 231001`.
- **What was not tested**: A live `addReactionFeishu` HTTP roundtrip to the actual Feishu Open Platform server with a paired bot token + chat_id is not exercised on the contributor machine (would need a configured Feishu workspace). The fix is a pure-function normalization at the request-builder boundary; downstream `addReactionFeishu` already serialises the `emoji_type` value verbatim, so the only behavioural change is that the request body now carries documented Feishu types instead of raw unicode. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live Feishu API recording is required; vincentkoc / ProjectClownfish already validated this fix shape via `pnpm -s vitest run extensions/feishu/src/reactions.test.ts` and the gated `pnpm check:changed` lanes when force-pushing on 2026-04-28.

## Test

- `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/reactions.test.ts` ŌĆö 5 cases (pass-through / case normalization / unicode conversion / whitespace trimming / unknown emoji fallback)
- `pnpm check:changed` ŌĆö green (per vincentkoc's 2026-04-28 status update)

Closes #66406

target.title: CLI: clarify gateway url override auth hints
target.body:
## Summary
- improve the gateway URL override auth error with source-specific follow-up hints
- tell CLI users they can remove `--url` when they meant the default local or SSH-tunneled gateway
- tell env override users to set `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN` or `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PASSWORD` alongside `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL`

## Why
OpenClaw already correctly rejects gateway URL overrides unless explicit credentials are provided, but the current error is still easy to stumble over in real Docker, VPS, and TUI flows.

This update keeps the same security behavior while making the failure more actionable:
- CLI `--url` users get a hint that they may not need `--url` at all for the default local or tunneled gateway
- `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL` users get a direct explanation that env URL overrides do not reuse config-only credentials and must be paired with explicit env auth

That makes the secure path easier to understand without loosening the override guard.

## Testing
- [x] `pnpm test -- src/gateway/call.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm test -- src/tui/gateway-chat.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm build`

## AI assistance
AI-assisted. I verified the final code change, reviewed the surrounding call paths, and ran the targeted tests and build locally.

commands.author: kumaxs

target.title: fix(cron): emit before_agent_reply phase unconditionally for cron triggers
target.body:
## Problem

Cron isolated-agent turns can abort with `externalAbort: true`, `stopReason: "aborted"`, and `error: "LLM request failed."` despite the model provider being healthy and no rate-limit hit. The abort fires after \~6 minutes, well within the configured `timeoutSeconds: 600` (10 min) wall clock.

See #93530 for full issue details.

## Root Cause

The cron watchdog (`createCronAgentWatchdog` in `src/cron/service/agent-watchdog.ts`) depends on the `before_agent_reply` execution phase to transition into the "executing" stage and clear its pre-execution watchdog timer.

In `runEmbeddedAgentInternal` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts`), the `before_agent_reply` phase was only emitted when `hookRunner?.hasHooks("before_agent_reply")` returned true. When no hook plugin registered for that event:

1. `runEmbeddedAgent` enters the cron-trigger branch
2. `hookRunner?.hasHooks("before_agent_reply")` returns false ŌåÆ phase is skipped
3. The watchdog never transitions out of "waiting_for_execution"
4. Pre-execution timeout fires (default: 60s)
5. `triggerTimeout()` ŌåÆ `runAbortController.abort(reason)`
6. The on-going LLM call is interrupted ŌåÆ `externalAbort: true` ŌåÆ "LLM request failed."

## Fix

This PR unconditionally emits the `before_agent_reply` phase for cron triggers, then separately wraps the hook execution in the `hasHooks` guard. This way the watchdog sees the phase even when no before_agent_reply hook is registered.

**Before:** `if (trigger === "cron" && hookRunner?.hasHooks("before_agent_reply"))`
**After:** `if (trigger === "cron")` (emits phase) + `if (hookRunner?.hasHooks("before_agent_reply"))` (runs hook)

## Testing

- Existing cron tests pass unchanged since the phase is now emitted strictly *before* the hook guard that was already there
- The watchdog pre-execution timeout path is exercised by `src/cron/service/agent-watchdog.test.ts`
- Manual verification: _pending_

Fixes #93530
target.author: kumaxs
